Name: 7_company_team_manager_working
Representative_Docs: ['hi everyone i m looking for advice on what to do about my current annoying situation as an intern during the holiday season i accepted an offer to work as an intern this summer at a very small start up it looked great their two full stack developers seemed super helpful and i was super excited to work with them i had very few skills back then and was over the moon that i had an internship offer at all the first red flag i wasn t going to get paid i was going to back out but because of good money coming from another project getting rejected elsewhere and the opportunity to work with a real team albeit small i stayed when i started i quickly found out that one of the developers had quit and a few weeks after that the other quit as well this left me as the only full stack developer there nice there s a mobile developer who is an amazing guy but knows surprisingly little about anything outside of swift ios dev and there are two developers who are entirely ai focused so we were left with this code base and i was the only one who knew how to read it update it etc the mobile developer does all the code reviews and he really tries to help but since he doesn t know what is going on it s really up to me to not make errors fix them when i always do at first it was a lot of stress but i also was getting exponentially better as a developer because of it i rose to the challenge and i enjoyed that for a bit however the joy has now worn off sigh as a result of my new position and responsibilities they keep telling me they are working to get me paid they have apparently applied to some grants for the money and if that falls through they say they will pay me out of their own pockets sigh however i honestly not sure if i believe them i see the way that they talk about people in the past and the way they conduct their business and they take advantage of everyone and everything their can interviewing people for free consults no intention of hiring making rude jokes about people right after they left a meeting etc i just have this rotten feeling that they are secretly getting me to work for free and stringing me along with the idea of getting paid just until summer is up they are also not easy to work for and in hindsight i should ve clued in when the others left we ll have meetings and talk about what moves to make going forward but it s all work i m going to have to do super weird having the room talk about things they don t know how to do and then turn to me and be like go then their wanna be ux ui designer it s not their official position will constantly ask me to develop new features and then get disappointed when it s not exactly their vision for example and i m going to be general with this example for identity reasons and such but there was this flow they wanted this huge form on our front end to go through it has a million dependencies of what shows up when and how many things can be clicked etc etc i built it and it was one of the harder projects i have ever done it works quite well and i am very proud of my work however i organized it such that it acts like nodes in a graph with each child node watching its parents tell it when to show up i m sure this is getting vague but the point was that this person an adult practically threw a fit that the dependencies didn t flow from parent to child i tried to explain how i thought this solution was more elegant but i gave up eventually they also got mad about inconsistent names across platforms when i was simply following their figma and documents that they made i was gonna make it consistent but i didn t want them to freak out about me using different labels backfired eh i asked them to bring on more people but we had interviews with them and i saw some of their work and so far i feel like working with any of them would be more of a headache than a help i m not a great developer but damn i saw some bad stuff they don t seem to have money to pay for any good developers outside of their ai projects so my questions to all you lovely people who decided to read all of this what is my best next move how do i bring up their current treatment of me i would quit but i m getting good experience working on big projects but i am teaching myself everything not getting paid yet despite constant promises and getting treated by this one person like a bit of their developer bitch i rather not stir up some bad blood because i feel like that would make my job harder too i m not a confrontation person either but i feel like they know that too i just have some tunnel vision about the whole thing and i would love the opinions from technical people thank you amp x b edit before anyone asks during the interview process i forgot a bit about asking about payment and i was just excited for my first internship offer plus unpaid internships are incredibly rare where i am and i m fairly sure illegal so i never really considered that i wouldn t get paid i was informed about this about two three months after accepting to join the team right as places stopped accepted applications and most places had their internships filled edit td lr i got myself into a shitty situation i m an unpaid intern working as the only full stack dev at this tiny tiny start up i m getting a lot of good experience working on big projects but other than that this position sucks and i m being treated poorly by one particular member i m also being told that i will get paid but i m sus if that is actually going to happen need help trying to find the next steps although it s pretty clear that the next step is run i just don t want to not have an internship for the last two months of summer i have become such a better developer because of this but im sick of feeling like i am being taken advantage of', 'soo i am a bit conflicted right now and need some advice i apologize if i jump around all over the place as i have been considering writing this up for some time now so i am basically unloading anyways i graduated with a bachelors in cs end of after months i got hired by a pretty well known consulting firm as a software engineer remote for now name starts with a t usually at this firm every new hire goes through an initial learning program usually weeks java and some go through an additional weeks of full stack maybe because i already had this experience i was not put into this program but got assigned straight to an account client whereas everyone else had to go through account interviews and had some say on what project or tech stack they wanted to work on anyways i got assigned to an account automatically its been months going on started march st i went through about a month of onboarding for the company and the client company which heavily emphasized agile development scrum throughout this time the project manager was telling me how i would be developing a little bit but mostly i would be fulfilling their business analyst role for the account project this seemed off to me so i had multiple talks trying to reconfirm this as i expected a software engineering junior dev role but he kept telling me that everyone on the account has to go through business analysis training so that everyone is on the same page anyways after the first month onboarding i went through about weeks of developer training on an itsm cloud platform called servicenow along with some random courses on itil and business analysis but most of the training was servicenow development this dev training left me very unfulfilled there was barely any programming just a small amount of scripts in js i guess i am too used to the programming assignments that i went through at university in c java and c after that weeks about weeks since i started i was told that i would start shadowing one of the senior devs who i would be helping with an integration project from servicenow to sap at this point another thing that caught me off guard was a team meeting where on a document everyones roles were listed next to my name was business analyst i talked to the project manager again and he said it was a typo and emailed me an updated file that said junior dev i guess at this point i should also say for the first two months i was allocated to the project already but it wouldn t show up in the system so i couldn t see it or the role i was assigned it took two and a half months to get allocated while being told hr says you were just allocated so give it a day or two to update and it wouldn t update then i would go and inform project manager again he would say he contacted hr and that it would happen shortly this happened close to times until i finally saw it in the system anyways a day or two after that the senior dev got covid and went on week leave during this time i basically had nothing to do i asked several times if there was stuff i could work on and the project manager assigned me random courses on servicenow machine learning and performance analytics never dabbled in machine learning or ai before i got these done in about days and afterwards i was told that i could just look at side courses or self learn until the senior dev came back i decided to review my old course notes at university and stuff regarding data structures and algorithms did that for about a week and the last days before the senior dev came back i ll admit i goofed off a bit now senior dev came back from leave around she had a lot on her plate i ll admit and told me that we would be starting the integration project shortly and told me to brush up on some stuff regarding integrations with servicenow did a small lab assigned by her that week and awaited more details and or work from her but she was busy the entire week so i decided to try to self learn again now for the last week and half i have been getting small half hour tasks every day or two that honestly anybody can do i have literally only written one line of code since mid may while being told that the integration project will be starting shortly up until today during this free time i have been trying to keep myself busy learn python and just self learn in general but this is driving me nuts i was barely getting any development experience with this platform servicenow and for the last weeks it feels like i have done nothing besides go into the system and change up some text for some labels i am considering talking to the group in the consulting firm that handles the allocation of associates to accounts but have been informed in the initial onboarding during the first month that you cannot get off an account or switch accounts in the middle of a project without it negatively affecting the associates internal resume on top of that apparently this project i have been assigned to has been in the works for years and they still expect another years according to the senior dev i am shadowing some people i know in the industry recommend that i should stop complaining and stick it out at this company as i am still getting paid decently for my first job post graduation without much work effort but i feel like i am stagnating on one hand it would look bad if i were to try to job hope only months into my first job but then it would also look bad imo if i work here for a year or two and barely get any programming experience i don t know what to do and am looking for some professional advice', 'here s my sankey diagram amp x b well it finally happened to me everyone after a long and arduous six years of undergraduate and graduate studies i can at last claim to have an offer of employment on my hands and a pretty lucrative one at that i ve lurked on this sub for a few years now but never posted before so wanted to give back to the community and tell my story i ll go through my job search process my results and insights and recommendations amp x b about me skippable i graduated from a top cs school in may majoring in math and cs looking back i definitely didn t invest as much time into applying for full time jobs as much as i should have over my years in undergrad i had rotated through a bunch of different internships from health insurance to consulting to financial services so when the time came to apply to full time jobs i honestly didn t really know where to go or even which industry i wanted to work in i had done a few online assessments but none of them ever led to anything or i was ghosted entirely i actually didn t even know about leetcode at the time so even if i wanted to do well most likely i would not have been prepared for the rigor of technical interviews that we are all faced with amp x b the first semester of senior year i took the natural language processing course at my university and ended up really enjoying it i found it to be a cool intersection of math cs and linguistics all of which i have been very interested in for as long as i can remember i really like learning foreign languages hence the linguistics part this course made me realize that i wanted to study machine learning and nlp in more depth leading me to apply to about different grad schools for cs i got into of them and selected the one which had the best reputation in terms of nlp research amp x b overall i think i made the most of my time in graduate school i currently work as a research assistant for a cs professor where i m enhancing nlp algorithms to help robots understand human commands i also participated in a hackathon hosted by our business school and my team won second place before covid hit i didn t have any internship secured for the summer so i wasn t affected as much as some other people whose offers got rescinded after consistent applying i ended up participating in a weird last minute summer program affiliated with harvard medical school where i worked as a data science intern helping the endocrinology team investigate the origins of thyroid cancer this was a cool summer project but it wasn t an internship that i could convert to a full time offer post graduation so i essentially had to start from scratch once the school year started hoping to land at least one offer before graduating with my masters in may of this year amp x b the positions i was targeting at first i was mainly looking at software engineer positions since it felt like they were a bit easier to prepare for thanks to sites like leetcode and pramp eventually however i shifted more to data scientist analyst roles despite the fact that interviews for these tend to be kind of a crapshoot in terms of difficulty the types of questions they ask etc the offer i ended up getting is more data driven which i think i m ultimately happy about because overtime i realized that i didn t really want to be a pure swe my strongest programming languages are java and python i had also done a software systems project in c where we essentially had to build gmail and google drive from scratch but projects like that never really clicked with me and i soon realized that i would much rather work in a data driven role rather than as a backend full stack engineer amp x b here are my job search stats beginning in early august and ending this week when i got my offer amp x b jobs applied i say because some companies i applied to more than once no response rejections including both immediate rejections and ones where i moved at least one step forward in the process online assessments including both leetcode style questions and data challenges first round interviews final round interviews both for data centric roles offer amp x b my strategy amp x b the websites that i used to apply were primarily handshake linkedin and indeed i applied to the company that i got my offer from through handshake if you re not familiar this is a great relatively new platform specifically for university students where you can apply to all kinds of jobs and internships as well as attend company events and career fairs linkedin and indeed are also great they were generally what i used to apply to internships back in undergrad in terms of the timeline i applied to a whole bunch of positions in august and september and after that my pace slowed down significantly both because of schoolwork and because around that time the market seemed to dwindle and there weren t that many new jobs posted of course we re in a pandemic so i have no idea what the timeline would be under normal circumstances but i would imagine it would be pretty similar in that most jobs would be posted late summer early fall and then it would slow down and different companies would have different timelines this sub tends to emphasize the value of networking but in my case i didn t really network all that much i did reach out to my connections at my university and send out a few cold emails to alumni and even got a couple of referrals e g pinterest amazon but strangely i did not even get an interview despite the referral again not sure if this is due to the pandemic and how things would have looked otherwise i will definitely try to network more when applying to positions in the future but it didn t really help me in any significant way this time around to prepare for swe interviews i mainly used leetcode and pramp looking at leetcode now which i actually haven t done in a few months lol since i ve been focusing on data roles lately i ve solved questions in total i didn t really have any consistent routine of questions a day or anything like that maybe it would have helped to do better in swe interviews but ultimately it didn t end up mattering since i moved away from applying to swe roles sometime in the fall i also completed all the questions available on pramp over the course of months or so i think this is a great free way to practice mock interviews if you want to land a swe job to prepare for data centric roles i had taken a big data analytics course back in fall so i rewatched those lectures and they proved to be quite helpful for those interviews i maintained a spreadsheet where i kept track of every job that i applied to and its current status i highly recommend this as it helps you remember where you re at in the process for different companies and it s also just fun to look at the statistics afterwards amp x b my offer amp x b company one of the big u s banks rhymes with bells cargo title quantitative analytics specialist location charlotte nc another option is dallas tx but i think i m going to go with charlotte either way it ll be remote until covid s over base salary relocation bonus i know this company might not have the greatest reputation in the banking industry especially with the whole faking accounts scandal they had a few years ago but the role entails exactly the kind of data driven work that i enjoy doing and i think that for a first full time job after graduation it s not at all a bad place to be plus the pay is good lol amp x b my interview experience amp x b i applied to said company at the end of september it took them months to get back to me and i had a first round phone interview in early december this went well i was asked pretty standard data science questions then at the end of january they sent us a case study data challenge which was also quite straightforward the final round consisted of two interviews a case study presentation with q amp a and another technical modeling interview similar to the first round amp x b i felt confident in my answers for both interviews which is a feeling i don t get very often but i think their questions weren t as difficult as some other companies tend to ask it also depends on the structure of the interview process and which kinds of interviews they throw at you for example i interviewed for a similar data driven role at another company also a bank and they also had me do a data challenge as part of the first round however the catch was that the final round involved two business oriented case study interviews which i was never good at and i feel like are designed more for people studying finance than swes or ml folk i didn t end up passing this interview but on the bright side it definitely gave me insight into the interview process for data centric jobs and ultimately helped me land the offer that i have now amp x b some closing tips and remarks amp x b make sure you have some hobbies other than cs programming that you can engage in during your free time i think it s impossible to keep applying to jobs without feeling the effects of burnout and this definitely happened to me a few times along the way so it s really important to do other stuff every once in a while personally i really enjoy music and tried to find some time to listen to it whenever i could this helped me relax and take my mind off things for a bit before continuing the grind as other people on this sub have pointed out please take care of your body sitting in front of a computer all day is super harmful and you need to stretch out and move around from time to time try to go outside at least for minutes hopefully this should be easier once the pandemic is over in my case i ve gotten into yoga over the past few years so i would try to take virtual yoga classes times a week this is a great way to counteract the sitting that you do throughout the rest of the day have a support system that you can reach out to in case you need help this is especially important right now and it s something that i m gradually learning the value of again i know we re in a pandemic but take a minute to call your friends and ask how they re doing or try to make new friends in communities where you share similar interests it s difficult to want to do this sometimes but reaching out to other people will help you feel less alone and remind you that there are other important things besides finding a job lastly as cliche as this might sound just keep your head up and keep moving forward and know that your mental and physical health should always come before that shiny new faang job things might look especially grim right now because of the times that we re living in but rest assured that sooner or later you will land that amazing role you ve been dreaming of and until then remember that you are more than just your resume you are a human who needs to have a life outside of school and work the best advice i can give is to simply persevere and look toward the future while also remembering that your present self even without a job should amount to more than enough amp x b if you read through this entire post thank you so much to everyone on this sub know that wherever you are in the job search you are awesome and i wish you the best of luck in the process']

Name: 8_ai_hate speech_metal_statement
Representative_Docs: ['agreed take as an example the algorithms that evaluate physical beauty during online video interviews clearly a bias in the hiring process and leaves collateral damage should be laws in place regarding other aspects of ai look at the findings of the fired google head of ai their research shows that it s very expensive and time consuming to train ai models and even after you have a model the expense is even greater to continuously monitor and verify the results it s much cheaper in practice to just have a person do the thing you want ai to do instead of always monitoring the ai for quality and then fixing mistakes when they happen', 'well now you re asking one of the big questions in cs the real answer is we don t know our version of ai today isn t really intelligence but fancy methods of generating algorithms they are really good but not general purpose intelligence in your scenario you might say trees are made of metal but that means the algorithm must have a concept of what a tree is and what metal is it must also be able to assess the meaning behind the statement because metal sculptures of trees exist so in a sense a tree can be made of metal but that Name: 10_resume_section_bullet_remove
Representative_Docs: ['you ve added skills in your education section you should instead have a dedicated skills section where you list all the languages libraries and tools you know you don t need to add context here project work in using feature branches etc just list the them this is important because the first person reading your resume will mainly care about whether you have relevant skills so they ll want to be able to refer to this list i d reduce your education section to one bullet for your gpa and one bullet relevant coursework ds amp a where you list any upper level electives i e machine learning databases for projects if you completed any of them recently soon before or since graduating i d list dates if they were all completed years ago better to leave them off for projects and experience you should use past tense start all of your bullets with an action word built used created led developed etc and use the star method this is harder for personal projects but do it if you can frame your sentences as what you did not what the project is i e change your job application quiz bullets to built a responsive used node js for the backend use one sentence per bullet and work on making your writing more formal this may mean dropping some information even though they re bullet points they should still essentially be full sentences i e if you were to add i before the action word it should be a full sentence finally as a personal preference of mine i d suggest a sans serif font but it s up to you', 'just took a look its not too bad some comments for your work experience projects you re missing numbers for example how much data did you ingest with your nlp model what was the accuracy etc in addition to above your main work experience is from your job as an application developer but its covering very little real estate on your resume i would flesh it out a bit again with numbers example how much improvement in time effort did you bring by automating data pipelines i would remove human in the loop part of the name of your project if my understanding is right the fact that its automated is more of the highlight i would remove the as i would move the education section near the top and either combine the work experience and projects section or put them next to eachother as a reviewer i have several seconds to see when where you graduated and what work you ve done when you have your education section in the middle it messes with the flow a bit also i would rename technical experience with projects and remove the projects indentation looking again its kind of weird how you have extracurricular which should be one word stuff in your employment section but you have another projects section i d just combine them i would add in a link to your linkedin page i might remove the award if you need more space languages and technologies section is ok but its taking too much space this is more for keyword searches by machines anyhow i would make it a single line with smaller font and use your real estate for better use lastly this has nothing to do with your resume but i would go to your alma mater s career fairs if you can edit i would google action words in resumes to see how you can improve some of the wording in your bullet points with a more active voice some of them are ok but some of them aren t makes a difference if you re actually looking out for them', 'this may be long because there s a lot to unpack here but i look at your resume and see glaring issues it s pages long and contains lots of filler content the first step you can take towards making your resume one page which is a surprisingly big deal is refactoring the theming you have going on there s a lot of unnecessary vertical space between bullet points your name amp info section could be condensed vertically and you re sacrificing of the width of the page to words some other formatting advice move all dates to the right side of the page i d move the education section to the bottom it s the least impressive part to me try not to use italics etc actually go to the bottom comment on this page and click that link for insight into the world of standardized formatting let s talk about the intro blurb at the top of your resume one thing i m noticing you do a lot is overexplain the details of your life i think you can assume that people will read your resume which makes that info largely redundant feel free to tell a story about your life in an interview or a cover letter but i don t think it has a place in your resume i e that info is already written down in a contextual manner no need to explicitly state it as i said earlier you should move your education to the bottom of your resume i also think you should remove the exchange because it s just taking up space i would recommend getting rid of the coursework section and have a coursework category in the education section like how languages is a category in the proficiency section only include relevant applied courses in the category e g machine learning and data mining algorithm design and analysis etc don t include the languages in which the courses were taught it s stated in your proficiency section speaking of your proficiency section rename it to skills imo the other items are somewhat implied and it s in your best interest to cut content there are these asides all over your resume that make you seem nervous skittish less competent remove all of the proficient unless otherwise indicated stuff incl working knowledge limited etc tbh this looks like self Name: 11_math_algebra_need_calculus
Representative_Docs: ['for some reason many non programmers think of programming as math maybe that was true in the s or before when computers were doing flight simulations or something but you generally need nothing more than algebra if there s math it s due to the programs you write not due to programming for example suppose you are writing programs to do a monte carlo simulation the program has math in it but you could write a program that plays tic tac toe that has no math because the program itself isn t about math many programs require much math what is more important is logical thinking attention to detail a good understanding of how a program works some subfields related to programming like machine learning ai cryptography etc do need math but again it s due to the programs you are writing and the subject area you are in not because programming inherently requires math to give a better analogy suppose you want to write a program to play chess or poker you could ask how much poker or chess do i need to know to program people would say that s ridiculous you don t need to know any of that but in reality if you want to write a program to play poker you have to learn the rules of poker and add that to your program it s not that you need to learn poker to program it s that you need to learn poker to write a poker program similarly you don t need to know much math to program but you need to know math to write a math program people often confuse the two if your job is about writing math programs then you need to know it if it s not then you don t some jobs require a fair bit of math some require nothing more than algebra and not even that what is useful is mathematical reasoning the kind of skills you need to think about solving math problems can be useful to program but most people call this logical reasoning or algorithmic reasoning and isn t strictly math but has math like elements', 'i think too many people think that programming is all about math yes in the old days that was probably true you watch say hidden figures and you needed math to compute rocket flight paths and such you got a computer because it could do these computations faster than humans could who were called computers but these days you have programs that let you write a blog which requires very little math do you think you need trigonometry and geometry to write a program for a blog these days you generally learn math because the skill to do math shows you can solve other kinds of problems not because geometry is directly applicable to programming indeed programming is more about remembering various details of your programs organizing your code in a way that you can find issues with your programs fix and modify it and have others be able to read and modify your code if the programs you write require math machine learning ai data science game programming scientific programming then yes you need math but only because the programs you write need math not because programming needs math for the most part you need basic math add subtract multiply divide and perhaps the ability to think mathematically proofs and such your ability to do math is more important than the actual math you are doing that you know how to do calculus problems is more important than the calculus problems you solve it s like saying learning chess can help you program if you can solve a chess problem it means you can reason out a solution that you are solving chess in particular is irrelevant to programming that s a difficult concept to process solving a problem any kind that involves reasoning is a useful skill and a skill that could translate to programming oh yeah they do say linear algebra is useful in game programming at least with some kind of physics engine etc', 'nowadays in even the subsection of math contains the amount of knowledge for the previous couple centuries it s due to one of physicists but i don t remember the name so it s a long way probably is by itself applied math just renamed into ai compression it s a task which attacked from convex optimization combinatorial optimization from another side gui in web or in standalone application no math i think physics engine you need linear algebra ode so it s hard a priori say which math do you need but very common math areas linear algebra graphs probability will allow you and then support you curiosity and study to some extend some subfields which is needed in your project']

Name: 16_web_web development_web dev_development
Representative_Docs: ['sure i ll have a go before the react revolution web dev meant building a real website where individual web routes resolved to individual web pages this was done using actual html files in a folder structure a flat file generator or using a cms like wordpress or drupal joomla a page was a page was a page and you could read the source of a site and see the actual content of it even if you were using ajax it was to fetch fully formed html not rest routes to get raw data this was because before the major js frameworks composing dom in the browser was quite laborious you could do it in jquery or maybe handlebars but only for small pieces not whole pages it was more efficient to just compose on the server side which was closer to the database anyway however i think what you are really getting at is that modern web dev seems to be only spas and raw data between server and client there s two reasons for this trends and money the trend is obvious js frameworks are very powerful incredibly liberating and fun to use once you get set up the ecosystem means there s probably a package for whatever you need to do meaning the time to results can be incredibly fast this is balanced by the fact that you can spend days hunting down package incompatibility issues in obscure tech threads but overall i think you end up ahead assuming you don t need to maintain it too long sure you are replicating pretty much all of the features of browsers for no reason but there s not much you can t do it s a bit like owning an instant pot once you have one you want to cook everything it even if it takes you twice as long my favourite example of this is the gatsby page builder born from a trend towards static site builder which were themselves due to a rediscovery of how good normal flat file sites could be without a db or js frameworks someone stuck a react frontend on it and now for some reason we ve come full circle when the whole point was to reject the spa ecosystem the second factor is web dev market and i think this is the bigger factor the cold truth is that the market for static websites where a spa is overkill is pretty much dead this is hard to hear for a lot of people because it was the bread and butter of a lot of developers for many years the fact is that it s virtually impossible to make a living wage out of building classic hosted html or cms based websites for example i ve been asked many times by friends and colleagues to help them set up a website for a business they started during covid shutdowns every single one of them i pointed to a site builder wix squarespace wordpress shopify between all the options available one suited their needs and for less than two hundred dollars a year they have a fully functioning site they can edit themselves every one of them was able to go from zero to a useful site with no web dev experience it looks good does what they need and costs them virtually nothing now consider if i had offered to do it for them i d probably use the same tools but have to charge to an hour for my time i d probably spend a week or more setting it up for them and end up billing them thousands of dollars what do they get for their money a site that looks exactly as they expect that they could have done themselves if they had tried my point is that the market for traditional websites is dwindling as the big builders get better and better wix has got an ai chat bot that frankly blows my mind it s so good at understanding what i want it s now at a point where i could not justify taking anyone s money let along a friend s for building a normal website as a result people like me have pivoted exclusively to building more complex apps that do justify the use of a js framework corporate applications saas products and bespoke integrations that need a presentation layer data and analytics are also a big one that s where the money is and that s why you are finding people are pushing you in that direction that s where the work will be in future', 'server side logic so everything i talked about above concerning stuff running in a user s browser or installed on a user s phone or pc is client side however modern applications almost always utilize server side logic and apis nowadays and this is distinctly seperate from the client you may have heard the terms front end engineer someone that deals with the client side app back end engineer someone that only deals with the server side or full stack engineer someone that works on both the front and back ends these terms come from the fact that some companies have teams that exclusively work on the client side or the server side because the client and server are distinctly seperate they don t have to use the same language s some random examples your client could be a web app built using html javascript and your server side could be running php your client could be an electron app using html javascript and your server could be running java your client could be a c application using qt for the ui and your server could be running javacript on node js node is an engine that allows javascript code to run natively on a machine outside of a web browser etc the only requirement of the server side of an application is that it be able to interpret http requests and return a response in a format the client will understand e g an api that returns results in json format while client side developers typically deal with stuff like making the app look good cross device compatibility etc typically server side engineers don t have to deal with any of that server side developers have to deal with issues like how to fetch and return data from databases efficiently making the api endpoints that can return the data the front end developers need access permissions and data security etc state management templates and package managers just quickly touching on some other things you mentioned you mentioned jsx i m gonna lump that in with the general concept of templating basically the how to indicate the way in which data should be displayed to a user problem which is common and there are various libraries across different languages to handle it you also mentioned redux which has a vue js equivalent called vuex i talked about how frameworks like react came to be above the desire to allow developers to just manipulate the data and have the ui updated automatically well developers ran into more problems with that concept namely that if you have a large application with data being read and manipulated in multiple places there s the risk of creating confusion on where the state of the application is being modified to help organize things and track state changes libraries like redux were invented finally package managers like npm node package manager lots of people put re usable code libraries online as open source for people to use on sites like github to handle installing these rd party libraries for use in your applications and to help you update them when new versions or security fixes are released most modern language have some sort of package manager tool some examples javascript npm or yarn python pip php composer rust cargo the final takeaway so to summarize all that basically what i m saying is that if you understand the basic components of modern app development you ll soon realize that many of those confusing terms you are running into are just various implementations of libraries to solve the same problems library a might solve the problem for python library b might be the solution for rust and libraries c and d might be competing solutions for javascript but at the end of the day if you understand the underlying problems being solved it won t matter what language you re dealing with and you ll be able to quickly equate a new library to something similar solving a similar problem you ve used in another language what s really important is just understanding the general process and the what why of the solutions in one language first for school javascript would be a great choice to do your web based projects in just because you can use the same language in your in browser spa using react vue as on your back end javascript running on node to query databases and send responses back via an api alternatively if you re heavily interested in ai python is another easy to use choice that can be used on the front or back end or if you d rather not use interpreted languages and want to learn a powerful language that s growing in popularity rust is another option although i think you d have a lot more issues using it in a class setting where you don t have a ton of time amp x b hope some of that helps clarify things give you direction', 'both machine learning and web development are such broad fields that it s hard to generalize in a way that really does them justice i m going to do my best to provide a general perspective based on what i ve heard from friends in the field and my own experience web development everyone s favorite punching bag for some reason people in cs have this superiority complex about web development and it gets this negative reputation because it s easy and even bootcamp grads can do it or something like that while there is a small grain of truth to this for the most part it s a pot of bullshit web development much like literally any other sub field of cs can be as easy or as difficult as the problems you re solving sometimes those problems can be easy in that you just need a basic page to display some information with html sometimes these problems can be difficult like serving millions of requests per second from a large set of distributed databases located all around the world technically both of the above examples are web development and the only stereotype about web dev as a career is that it s easier to break into than other sub fields because you can learn the basics via a short bootcamp instead of a year degree but make no mistake your average bootcamp grad probably wouldn t be able to work on the second example problem above generally speaking the day to day of a web developer involves building websites and or the backend technologies that power them some of the easier to break into work in this subfield that requires less of a algorithms or math background is the design and front end portion of the stack where one is designing the look and feel or user interface and experience of the site using front end languages like html and javascript typescript and popular frameworks like react and angular etc some examples of more technical work that are still web development are generally more on the back end this can involve a wide range of languages anything from javascript to python or c and generally speaking the day to day work involves creating the server side system that the front end interacts with to store the users state as well as interactions with a oftentimes separate database service to read and write data there s a bit of overlap here with cloud engineers because these servers generally run on cloud services and while i wouldn t lump them in together cloud related skills using aws azure gcp etc can be useful for a back end developer to have you ll also often hear the phrase full stack web developer thrown around and all it means is that it s someone who is comfortable with the languages and tools used for both the front and back end development a good way to get a feel for this kind of development is to build your own full stack web project from scratch which is something you can easily find tutorials for on the internet machine learning riding the hype train arguably one of the most hyped sub fields of computer science machine learning is also an incredibly broad sub field in which the day to day work can vary greatly in addition it has a reputation of being fairly difficult to break into unlike web development because of the high math technical background required more on this in a minute i also personally think that the day to day work of an mle is one of the most misunderstood things and that most students tend to think it s far more glamorous than it actually is but that s neither here nor there the best way to describe the day to day work of an ml engineer is to break out what types of work require certain levels of education a lot of people who are into machine learning really want to be doing the cool stuff which usually means they want to be implementing deep learning models using a framework like pytorch or tensorflow the reality is that a fresh grad role doing any kind of ml engineering is unlikely to have very much model implementation if any in addition most roles and companies don t really require sexy deep neural networks for their data analysis and generally speaking in normal industry jobs you ll be working with linear models fancy linear models and decision trees in addition most of the work of an ml engineer actually has less to do with ml and more to do with data think of it this way if you re working on a team at a large company you ll only really need to experiment write the model once but you will need to re train the model with more new data all the time so most of the work that actually goes into running production grade ml at scale is around building data pipelines to train your existing models faster and better and with inference pipelines to serve the machine learning models to your end users the actual underlying model architecture and code is likely written once and the person doing that part of the job will probably have a phd so realistically working as an ml engineer with only an undergraduate degree ends up being more like working as a data engineer building data pipelines for training and inference of ml models at scale the core languages are python and hot take here sql there s this awesome blog post by vicki boykis that i think does a far better summarizing than i can here so i ll just link it but it s really fantastic and you should give it a read if data science ml is something you re interested in a note on salaries you specifically asked about salaries so i ll just comment briefly at the high end big n everyone makes a lot of money and it s mostly the same the ml folks might make a little bit more just because the demand is kind of high right now but i think some really specialized web dev folks are probably competitive generally speaking at big n the salaries are determined by your level and not your specific sub field role however once you go outside of big n i think there s a lot more room for variability ml is sexy right now so it will probably pay more on average but again that s a hugely sweeping generalization pay also fluctuates with the location quite a bit your best bet for getting good salary info once you have an offer are and the cscq salary sharing threads my own experience i don t have a graduate degree yet i work in ml at a self driving car company obviously this is in stark contrast to what i said above but i m the exception not the rule as almost all my colleagues have graduate degrees i was lucky to stumble on the opportunity i did and was uniquely prepared because i had done robotics ml research during my undergraduate education i don t think that my own experience constitutes the average undergrad experience trying to break into ml data science disclaimer please remember that i m making broad stroke generalizations that do not reflect everyone s experience i also obviously don t work in web development myself and my own role in ml is pretty unique take everything i wrote with a pinch of salt']

Name: 18_python_language_used_languages
Representative_Docs: ['i have been a software developer for quite some time now i have sort of a unique upbringing into programming by means of geospatial technology which mainly uses python amp javascript via the time i served in the army with that being said i have worked in a multitude of projects that range from web development to intense detection based ai software since then i have taken to learning c with all of this experience you would think that i should be flying online tutorials and documentation but you would be wrong as it turns out i only had a superficial understanding of the more advanced oop topics i am not writing this to scare anyone away from learning c because it is one the fastest and most versatile languages used to this day despite its age i am writing this more so because i have not seen it said anywhere else besides a few youtube comments understand that c was created with the intention of being extremely versatile essentially giving developers infinite resources to create anything they want ie adobe apps video games etc and because of this there is a steeper learning curve in the case of understanding the core of oop topics there are many things that python and java keep under the hood like the concept of passing variables as a reference or by value this concept is not really talked about in regards to other languages because python programmers do not have the ability to manipulate the method in which their variables are passed as parameters and arguments to other functions as a c developer you have complete and total control of scope within your program and this idea evolves into so many more concepts like pointers inheritance and polymorphism python certainly has these functionalities but not in the same way c implements them my advice to anyone looking to learn their first programming language is to pick up python since again it keeps a lot of stuff under the hood this will allow you to understand the basics of oop and begin building programs more fluidly without the headache of having to understand the nitty gritty if you have already started learning c first off i understand your pain and secondly give me a shout if you re stuck i may be able to help you edit learning c or c will provide you with substantial knowledge of oop ultimately knowledge is power and learning this language will provide you with a greater ability to problem solve i am not recommending to never learn c if you don t you ll be missing out on the many of the fundamentals of oop that could help you debug i know that if i were to learn c first i would have grown exceedingly frustrated with the nitty gritty and could have potentially quit programming my recommendation to the impatient type of person like myself is to learn python or javascript first because it can get you building quicker', 'so long as microsoft still exist and they don t make another language to replace c it will remain huge the main downside back in the day was that if you programmed in c it meant you were mostly stuck in the windows world this is no longer an issue since net core is cross platform and can be used outside of windows plus it helps to have a cool rich editor such as vscode which integrates well i currently use c netcore vscode monogame on linux ubuntu and it works like a charm you can pretty much do a lot of things with c web apps desktop apps mobile apps games etc i know you mention python so consider these things c is much faster than python since it s a compiled language not interpreted c is better suited for bigger projects since it s statically typed it s actually both static and dynamic but it s always best to use it as a statically typed language with dynamically typed languages it gets harder as the projects get bigger and more complex on the flip side python is much better suited if you re writing smaller scripts in terms of style c is in a class with the likes of c and java where as python being a scripting interpreted language is comparable to languages such as javascript ruby lua etc in terms of web development i think there are enough frameworks for both languages to make it a wash other than that the only things python is better suited than c is if we re talking about machine learning or general scripting the rest such as desktop apps mobile apps games etc i heavily favor c anyways if you re planning to stay in the data science industry then stick with python and c c but keep in mind that if you can program in c jumping to other languages like c or java shouldn t be an issue', 'programming in c without ever making use of the low level stuff misses the point for just about any task you could imagine there are languages that are better than c in most ways more cross platform more and better runtime libraries more concise more expressive easier threading easier threading so why do people still use c because when it comes to raw performance c blows all of them out of the water sometimes maximum performance and minimum memory usage are important for example in operating system kernels game engines browser engines mathematical computation physics simulations device drivers real time robotics machine learning and so many other important areas when you need that performance and memory only languages that compile to native code and let you have control of memory management will do because any other language will get in the way and limit how much things can be optimized and of the small number of languages that compile to native code and let you have control of memory management c is the most powerful expressive and powerful by far aside from maybe c c is great but doesn t give you a lot of tools for scaling to really large programs pascal delphi haven t been popular for decades rust is rapidly growing in popularity and getting better fast but it s got maybe of the mindshare and there s maybe of the amount of useful code libraries that c has today there are a handful of others that are even less widely used so sure you can try to write clean high level c code like python code but it misses the point the reason people use c is because they need the ability to optimize the most important parts of the code and those important parts need low level memory management complex templates etc']

Name: 20_students_edx_teacher_teachers
Representative_Docs: ['that s quite possible you sound like you re a smart kid and you ve clearly got a bright future ahead of yourself the fact that you care enough to make a post like this bodes well for your future and your ability to calmly take push back on your statements and reevaluate your position is something the vast majority of adults never really figure out in my experience kudos for being mature and intellectually aware enough to take feedback without getting defensive that s a rare trait at any age and a hallmark of intelligence i d like to give you a bit of advice sorry for the length a small change in perspective will fix this problem for you and supercharge your ability to learn effectively it certainly did for me your teacher may well be ineffective lazy etc that being said that s also good practice for university the best way i ve heard that style of teaching described is benign neglect while it often isn t a pleasant experience from a student perspective because you feel like you have to do everything on your own it s actually a great learning experience and generally leads to great outcomes for students that are willing to put in the effort to handle things mostly on their own consider the following question why do you actually care about the results of this class is it because you need to pass it or because you want to actually know this stuff if you actually care about learning it no teacher good or bad can stop you from learning it all they can do is either help or fail to help i d suggest you reframe the entire topic of school education learning in your head it ll do wonders for both your ability to learn and your overall outcomes it sounds like you were expecting the traditional sage on the stage approach from your teacher where they tell you what s worth paying attention to and learning and your experience is largely one of listening and memorization in this situation the teacher is the driver and you re in the passenger seat when you encounter a teacher that doesn t drive it can feel jarring and worrying how are you supposed to know where the goal line is and get there when the teacher refuses to drive the answer is to put yourself in the driver s seat don t be a passenger in your own education it s not your fault if this is what you re used to this is pretty much the norm for education across the vast majority of the world students are taught through environment and experience that if they just show up follow the rules and do what teachers ask then at the end of the process they ll be educated and they ll have grades to prove it this is a bad way to think about learning because it s too important to leave to others the educational system is slow to update resistant to change and an ineffective teacher can mean no progress if you sit around waiting for them to start driving when they never will grades are just extrinsic motivators only useful within a school they re made to seem the most important things in the world during school but they re absolutely useless once you get to the real world and most research shows pretty conclusively that they re actually harmful to the learning process overall especially when compared to learners that are intrinsically motivated the solution here is to actively participate in the process of your education make decisions about what you plan to learn in each class what are your goals what do you already know what do you want to know that you don t already know what are your unknown unknowns and how will you discover them hint mentors and professors help greatly with this one by undergrad some students figure this out and the ones who don t just graduate and move into the work force in graduate programs the majority of students remaining now understand this is the best way to learn at the top of the pyramid this style of learning is the only option available pretty much any learning you do in a phd program is exclusively you in the driver s seat you decide what you re specializing in what to study how to research it and how to explain it with a dissertation if you approach education as a process that is on rails and you re just along for the ride then there s no guarantee you ll get what you need out of it maybe you get a good teacher and have a smooth ride or maybe you have a teacher that doesn t feel like driving if you don t have a plan then all you ll ever learn is whatever they decide to teach if you do then you ll learn to be systematic researching and making learning goals and then creating and executing a plan to get there the best part about this is that when you have a good teacher you ll learn much faster and in a much more exciting fulfilling way with yourself in the driver s seat because they can help you navigate and support you as needed i didn t learn this attitude until i decided to switch careers i was a hs literature teacher for years got fed up and decided to retrain and get a job as a scientist studying ai when i was in school the first time i expected teachers to do the heavy lifting and educate me i didn t make it particularly easy for them but even if i had i would have gotten middling results at best the second time around as an adult going back to school i had a lot more on the line there was a high bar to become an ai researcher with a big prize for success interesting job lots of and a dismal outcome for failure can t pay my mortgage used all my savings to go back to school teaching certs lapsed while in school failure meant unemployment and possible homelessness this reframed the entire process of learning in my head i d seen too many bad teachers to gamble on sitting still waiting for them to pour everything i needed to know into my head instead i decided i would do whatever it took to learn all i needed to know regardless of how good or bad my teachers were good teachersmay boost my speed but the bad ones didn t slow me down the worst they can do is not boost my speed reframing my learning process like this worked wonders i m now done with retraining and successfully working as an ai researcher at a large tech company with a salary that pays me more in a month than my entirely yearly salary as a teacher some states in the us pay teachers criminally low salaries mosy importantly i never stopped making learning goals and working towards them being able to teach myself has been an absolute boon i know what i care about knowing whether it s because of professional need or personal interest being purposeful about learning and knowing how to effectively research and teach myself has given me a huge competitive advantage at work and i ve realized that when done correctly i actually really enjoy learning something my younger self would have disagreed with vehemently', 'i know most people seem to hate the online classes the students the parents and us teachers we all hate it but even before the pandemic i thought that education would eventually start moving in this direction covid merely gave us all that big push before anyone could adequately prepare for it logically speaking in the age when everything is moving to the internet and our whole world is rapidly becoming digitized one would have thought that education would have taken this leap far more quickly than it has however as ever the world of education is one of the last to embrace change sure there are a few notable exceptions to this the teach now teacher certification program has made waves for being basically the first to offer teacher training online not requiring any in person sessions other than going to a praxis testing facility being ahead of the curve as they were they ve experienced exponential growth in the last years and they continue to be forerunners in the online only classrooms however when you look around there simply aren t that many other programs or schools doing this even over a year into the pandemic now i was never one who fully bought into sugata mitra s hole in the wall experiment and his claim that learning requires no teacher the role of the teacher is something i see as intrinsic to education at least until ai can do our jobs better than we can i do however think that learning from home in an online setting has quite a few advantages over the traditional classroom the biggest advantage is the one i ll start with that being it takes a major load of the responsibility for a child s learning off the teacher s shoulders and puts it back onto the students and their parents in the last years or so more and more responsibility has been shifted to teachers in terms of educating children while parents and the students themselves take less and less with online learning from home if falls to the students to step up they must stay on task they need to focus they need discipline so they re not using other apps and programs off screen when they should be learning if they can t do these things the teacher sitting across the city from them is powerless to enforce it thus the student will either fail or the responsibility falls to their parents to enforce these things this frees up the teacher to focus on actually teaching other advantages include easy access to technology less time to commute less money spent by the teacher to supply students with materials more independence on the part of students less stress in public speaking and social interaction for shy students voices given to more students instead of just dominant ones and more safety in terms of school violence and inappropriate interactions you might be thinking that none of these so called advantages are actually present at all and you are probably right however i think that has more to do with the fact that we are still in a transitional period where we haven t quite figured out how to make it work parents haven t figured out how to step up and take on the added responsibilities students haven t realized yet that they need to take more initiative and independence in their study teachers haven t figured out how to deliver lessons and use all the new technology effectively schools haven t figured out the best programs policies and procedures they should be using in short because we were all dumped into this situation with little preparation we don t know what we re doing and many are wrongly simply trying to wait it out so they can get back to normal the most forward thinking schools will be working on all of these things rather then just trying to get through so we can get back to normal i believe the schools which really tackle learning from home and hybrid classrooms are going to be the ones which get a head start going forward they will be the ones pioneering and getting things figured out and giving the best returns on student education while most lag behind and learning takes a large dip for several years likewise i think the teachers who really dig in and find a way to make all this work will have the easiest time transitioning into the new world of education which we are rapidly approaching', 'this needs some more nuance mit s announcement of this in june does a better job mit and harvard agree to transfer edx to ed tech firm u two part transaction would turn edx into a public benefit company while generously funding a nonprofit dedicated to strengthening the impact of digital learning mit and harvard university have announced a major transition for edx the nonprofit organization they launched in to provide an open online platform for university courses edx s assets are to be acquired by the publicly traded education technology company u and reorganized as a public benefit company under the u umbrella the transaction is structured to ensure that edx continues in its founding mission and features a wide array of protections for edx learners partners and faculty who contribute courses in exchange u will transfer net proceeds from the million transaction to a nonprofit organization also led by mit and harvard to explore the next generation of online education backed by these substantial resources the nonprofit will focus on overcoming persistent inequities in online learning in part through exploring how to apply artificial intelligence to enable personalized learning that responds and adapts to the style and needs of the individual learner the nonprofit venture will be overseen by a board appointed by mit and harvard and its future work will draw on ideas from current edx partners as well as mit and harvard faculty celebrating the past building a new future in a letter to the mit community sharing the news president l rafael reif noted that edx has engaged partner institutions reached more than million learners and exceeded million course enrollments all of us can take pride in what edx has accomplished including and perhaps most significant edx s role in helping create a thriving market for online learning he wrote the transaction announced today was spurred by rapid and profound shifts in that online learning market over the past months with the onset of the global pandemic and the accompanying surge in remote learning publicly traded ed tech firms have attracted major investment making it increasingly challenging for a nonprofit to keep pace almost a year ago recognizing this disruptive transformation of the market the edx board began to explore what it would take to sustain edx s mission in the future reif acknowledged in his letter that the initial expression of interest from u took some getting used to but following several months of reflection debate and negotiation he said the parties found an agreeable path a future for edx as a public benefit company that will pair the resources of a for profit player with a formal mission to serve the public good encoded into law in most states benefit companies emerged about a decade ago as a legally protected way for companies to commit to the public good the designation allows a commercial venture to seek to make positive impacts for societal stakeholders while also serving company shareholders familiar examples of benefit companies include patagonia kickstarter and ben and jerry s mit s own innovation fund the engine is also a benefit company the future of open edx if the deal is approved by state regulators u will pay million for the right to adopt edx as a subsidiary and take on the current portfolio of edx course offerings partner arrangements and base of users however the open edx open source software platform upon which edx and more than other learning sites around the world are based would not be transferred to u the nonprofit would own and enhance open edx ensuring its continued wide availability including its ongoing use by edx mit will continue to offer courses to learners worldwide via edx as well as on a new platform now known as mitx online mit s office of digital learning will build and operate mitx online as a new world facing platform based on open edx that mit is creating for mitx moocs mit faculty may choose to continue to offer their courses through the new edx after the transaction is completed or move them to mitx online under the terms of the transaction partner edx universities would have the option of continuing to offer their courses under the same terms as their current agreements the transaction imposes no restrictions on these institutions freedom to offer their courses through other means or to depart edx other protections for learners partners and staff in an array of provisions within the negotiated agreement u has embraced edx s mission and agreed to data protections for everyone who has used edx to date more than million learners as well as data usage standards that would protect new edx users as part of these provisions u has agreed to limit its use of sensitive individualized information based on learner choice basic contact information would be available to u for learner communications and learners will have the option to continue learning with edx or opt out and have all their information including basic contact data permanently deleted among other agreements as part of the deal u has also committed to guarantee affordability through the continuation of a free track to audit every course protect the intellectual property rights of member partners that contribute moocs ensure that participating colleges and universities may continue under their standing agreements with edx protect the privacy of individual data for all learners who use the edx platform work toward launching affordable stackable modular bachelor s degrees built from microbachelors programs participate actively in improving the open edx code to improve engagement pedagogy learning outcomes and blended learning features increase the number of courses available in non english speaking markets continue to offer a broad range of courses across disciplines provide accessibility features for learners with disabilities and host all courses that a member institution chooses to put on the edx platform finally u plans to retain all current edx employees who do not remain with the nonprofit continuing under harvard and mit s leadership staff will remain in their current roles and teams for the immediate future as u determines the ideal timeline and plan for bringing the two organizations together the transaction was approved by the edx board as well as governance bodies at mit and harvard it is expected to close this fall subject to customary closing conditions and certain regulatory and governmental approvals including approval by the office of the massachusetts attorney general the launch of a refreshed mission to reimagine learning as was the case with edx the resulting nonprofit will continue to be a free standing organization with a board appointed by mit and harvard however it will have significantly more funding and its mission will differ while edx will continue under u as an aggregator of university level courses the nonprofit will instead focus on the development of more personalized and responsive learning experiences that can be applied across the educational spectrum from high school to community college to adults trying to create opportunity for themselves in a changing economy the nonprofit will invest in research fund pilots and promote the adoption of insights across the education spectrum it will also seek out collaborations and aim to fund local partners to more effectively serve students from disadvantaged backgrounds as well as identify how to most effectively blend digital tools with in person support to help students learn particularly those for whom online learning has been more a promise than a reality the continuation of a vision over its nine years edx has achieved remarkable success in advancing its vision of making high quality university courses available to people everywhere with some courses offered and more than million certificates issued to learners edx has helped build a booming marketplace for college level content it has also pioneered formal university credit for some of its offerings including innovations such as the micromasters and microbachelors credential programs the willingness of u to help us create a unique deal presents exciting possibilities says sanjay sarma mit s vice president for open learning and the fred fort flowers and daniel fort flowers professor of mechanical engineering it gives us a chance to do something we had aimed to do from the start create breakthroughs that advance how teaching and learning take place to be honest as good as online education has become it s still not easy to sit in your kitchen or bedroom and take a college class reif adds with this move the educational nonprofit will develop new tools and techniques to make learning more personal more meaningful and easier for people all over the world at any stage in their lives careers or preparation that was the road we set out on with edx and this represents the next stage in that journey']

the year do you apply for internships are summer and spring internships the same length thank you for your time', 'if you haven t already checked it out here s my post on summer programs in general check it out to get an general framework for evaluating summer programs on your own if you re interested in engineering or technology you ve come to the right place again i apologize that many of these programs application deadlines have passed but this year has been quite difficult for organizing summer programs given the covid situation students interested in engineering and computer science also don t have the opportunity to really explore these subjects sufficiently in high school if you re lucky your school has engineering courses that you can take as electives or computer science courses beyond ap comp sci most students interested in engineering and cs have had to be resourceful in finding opportunities to confirm their interests or hone their skills you know the type the self starters who taught themselves to code since they were in elementary school they re serial hobbyists interested in robotics d printing arduino projects or cryptography but what if you have tech engineering interests and don t fit this mold if you come from any kind of underrepresented minority in stem woman black latino native american there are often even fewer opportunities and resources to ever get involved in python coding or first robotics competitions luckily there are a number of summer programs that try to address these inequalities in opportunity they are often free programs some great lists for computer science and math internships and research programs that have some overlap with some programs on this list overall if you re a engineering tech or cs interested student we advocate more than anything to get your hands dirty by working on independent projects completing online courses and just generally taking charge of your education the following list represents engineering technology and computer science programs that most top schools have heard of to get a better idea as to the competitiveness and admissions value of each program i ve tiered the programs into three categories s tier a tier and b tier s tier must go if admitted minority introduction to engineering and science mites program dates cancelled application deadline cancelled cost free
