Name: 28_words_word_dictionary_sentence
Representative_Docs: ['yeah you re right i can use an algorithm that searches through the dictionary i was also thinking about machine learning but like you said words are made up there s not even a way for a human to know a word is real until they look it up in a respectable dictionary i am also wondering if there is a way for the computer to look at the full sentence the same way a human looks at a sentence and goes hmm these words look familiar i wonder if i can split them up and then they split it up and any words that aren t recognized are sent through the dictionary to see if they re real then again names many non english names are spelled however people want them to be and unique characters are a problem lol', 'i d start with python easy to learn and pretty decent for nearly everything as for the sonnet i would store a list of words of verbs nouns etc in their own dictionary a list of keys that correspond to specific values so dict key value key and value can be of any type which is useful here this would let you input each words classifying it as you go and store it alongside the number of syllables or whatever it is you need i know shakespeare used syllables per line with alternating intonation then you would be able to randomly choose verbs and nouns from the dictionary i would use random module with key kys random randrange len kys where kys noun keys this chooses a random number in the range of a list of keys from whichever dictionary and gets the key at that index in the list you can use that word and then if you need the number of syllables you use noun key for now i would start with just using the same filler words and then using some sort of random word generator afterwords if you truly have that much time to commit to this it would be cool to build some sort of neural network ai that you train on sonnets that then makes it s own sonnets which should be pretty good if you tune it right this is pretty extreme i would say but you would definitely know a lot more programming', 'that s actually a pretty big field called natural language processing about which i m actually really fascinated but also about which i unfortunately know very little one thing you might try is using something really simple like a markov chain to do predictive analysis in short a markov chain does the following take a sample work this is usually done with text but it can also be adjusted to work with visual art or even sound the work must be subdivisable into integral parts for writing this usually means words subdivide the work for each unique word in the work build up a frequency list of how often that word is followed by any other word for example for the us declaration of independence the word we is followed by the word have four times and by other words times as such the odds of we being followed by have are or approximately now just reverse this process pick a random starting word and then pick a weighted random following word using your frequency list from step with a big enough sample your resultant text will be eerily similar to what you put in for example i once fed a python based markov constructor the complete works of shakespeare what it printed out not only had shakespearean language lots of thees and thous and forsooths but also felt like shakespeare the dialog spoken by my auto generated characters had the bard s dramatic tone with characters sounding melancholy or hamlet esque i d also be pretty interested if i could find a good api for this with looking at the phonemic frequency distributions amongst various languages in other words for a given language what sounds are most common to that language what phonemic flavors make up english vs japanese vs spanish']

Name: 30_neuroscience_psychology_neuro_psych
Representative_Docs: ['psychology professor here between the four none of them can get you a well paying job with just an undergraduate degree psychology a master degree in clinical related psychology can allow you to get counselor type jobs but to become a psychotherapist you need to get a doctorate in clinical psychology this route pays well a psychotherapist can easily get figure income a school counselor can get to the upper figures if you are not into counseling there is also io psychology that is more related to a business degree you can work in human resources or as a consultant also pays well if you want to stay in academia and do research e g a professor then a doctorate in any field of psychology would do but the pure faculty route might be the least paying path most assistant professors in psychology start at about to depending on the type of school and the average cost of living of the location cognitive science in general this would be similar to point above you would mostly be doing research in this path but depending on the kind of research you do some are very cutting edge artificial intelligence and others not so much but no matter what you need to be awesome at statistics and programming to do well here neuroscience the same as above although this has a heavy biology crossover so you can lean toward medical science or biology research if you desire to do that you want to major in biology premed and take a minor in neuroscience if there is one if there isn t then take whatever courses that are related to neuroscience sociology you can become a sociologist phd in sociology and do research and teach same as point for psychology but on average sociology professors make a bit lower than psychology professors you can go for the social work route i personally don t know how much these jobs pay master in social work can get you a social worker job doctorate can get you a director type job or allow you to become a faculty a bachelor degree in sociology can help you apply for law school many law students major in sociology history philosophy political science etc highest paying career path but this is not technically sociology anymore hope this helps', 'i have a ba in psych almost a bs but needed to graduate i have a master s in more or less experimental interdisciplinary studies mostly cognitive science minus the computer science i was hoping to pave the way towards a phd but with my student debt was limiting myself to competitive programs with full tuition coverage acceptance rates for these programs are more competitive than medical school i worked with a vanderbilt grad as a research assistant who was also a consultant to the a amp e show hoarders i did not get accepted and things got super dark for me multiple people suggested a career change and it infuriated me at first but on paper the average years ago starting salary was k so your number there i m also in the south seems low esp because of the number of remote jobs now and how covid accelerated that number average salary avg not starting for clinical psych phd was like ish at the time if i got in if i finished in years if i could start join a practice vs years and when i finished i would have a job which averaged just shy of the alternative with a much higher ceiling with only a bachelor s now my story might be different in that i found cs and software engineering to be quite analogous my psych studies drew me towards cognitive neropsych so computer as an analogy for brain activity and i was thinking about how to program a nn before i knew nn s were a thing and was naturally drawn towards ai as in the algorithms which mimic human behavior i e not data science and ml dl of which those algorithms are only a subset of all ai has to offer i also spent most of my master s studies investigating strategies for personal development and so i continue to apply and share these soft skills with others and attempt to crush it every day and then the depressing side i learned during my master s is the atrocious success rate of the western psychotherapeutic psychopharmacological approach in many areas while there are some miracles as a result of modern psychiatry take for example the success rates of ptsd treatment addiction treatment depression anxiety etc the only psychopharm hope for these might be work coming out of maps and johns hopkins but it s still too early to tell whether or not someone is going to ruin these as options all that and then as a psych professional dealing with the apa while also having to work with insurance companies sounds like a nightmare to me anyway off my soapbox i also continue to study team dynamics leadership and other areas related and influenced by psychology studies just to keep my brain fully lubed so that s me for you if you could make it with a double major you wouldn t close any doors all fields have needs for software skills computational neuropsych is an example imho a psych degree is relatively worthless until you get a master s minimum speech pathology was not on my radar though and i know someone in that field and it does seem to be maybe not so bad i do not know edu reqs tho so keep in mind the long term when making a final decision can you find similar interests and passion in cs by applying analogy what is interesting about psych and what is not interesting about cs can you take what you know about psych and improve your productivity as a programmer are you ready to continue to graduate school and even maybe a phd so you re looking at another years are you passionate about helping others improve their life situations i am are you potentially naturally gifted in helping others improve their life situations i am most definitely not or are you passionately driven towards science and research desire to write grants papers and conduct research you could research either psych or cs or both depending on the professor maybe your answers are different and maybe you should stick to psychology good luck', 'hello i m in desperate need for advice from people who have more experience than me in the field particularly cog sci cs ai cog neuro i would really appreciate any input i come from a math biomed background did a master s in computational neuroscience and during that time i exposed myself to neuro ai related research after more of my own research reading attending seminars though i found myself most intrigued by cog sci related topics in particular i ve been really interested in understanding and replicating human level intelligence in artificial agents and the questions regarding knowledge representation and learning in humans so after graduation i applied to do a phd in the us in computational cognitive science in psychology departments i didn t get into any of the programs i applied to i turned away to look for jobs in the industry for a bit but realized soon that the jobs that i can get with my qualifications so far don t interest me so right now i m a bit torn i think it s obvious that i have to continue my studies to end up doing what i want to do to be honest i don t even have a clear picture of what i want to do after grad school perhaps because i don t fully know what options are available in the industry all i can think of is deepmind google but i don t think i want to continue on in academia but if i wanted industry jobs i think i d be better off approaching my area of interest through cs the downsides of this option is that on top of what will already be two gap years by april i ll have to spend more time doing another short undergrad master s to build up comp sci coursework to even qualify do a phd in cs i ve never been fully detached from some kind of biomed throughout undergrad and master s so i fear that the coursework will be a waste of time if i realize pure cs isn t my thing i am aware that everyone has their own timeline and i should focus on my own i guess it just doesn t help when i see friends who are almost finished with their phds or mds while i feel pretty lost overall and then there s the option of applying to cognitive neuroscience labs i am based in canada and mostly looking in canada at the moment but open to international options i regretted not having looked more broadly last time around when applying because maybe computational cognitive science is a pretty niche field at the moment in any case i ve been trying to read more into the field s research but i can t seem to find research labs that align with what i ve mentioned perhaps my research interests in learning and reasoning knowledge representation is too broad i ve been trying to read more into areas like visual intelligence and language learning they re pretty new to me so i m having trouble absorbing info but i can probably get myself to like them more my biggest problem with this option though is that i can t picture my job prospects down the road in these cases i really wish i had a clearer bigger picture of things overall in terms of potential industry career options and of the current state of the art in research i feel like i m always behind with relevant research even though i try to keep up and that there s something i m not looking into to get me where i need to be i thought i might ask here in the hopes that someone might be able to offer their insights for example am i overlooking some cog neuro research that would align with my interests is there something i could do to broaden my research perspectives in general as for industry jobs what are some options i m not aware of and what do you think is the best way to get there once again i would appreciate any sort of enlightenment or guidance thank you so much for reading this far tl dr comp neuro grad interested in cog sci ai research needs advice choosing between cs job prospects but takes more time and cog neuro more relevant to prev research but job prospects unknown for phd or other options']

Name: 37_plagiarism_student_committee_assignment
Representative_Docs: ['i ll try to keep this brief tldr at bottom we were allowed to chose groups of and tasked with completing different case studies for once i was able to surround myself in a group of people who would pull weight so i thought the assignments were trivial and operational honestly would ve taken a single person no more than minutes to complete so for the first assignment me and group member a complete the assignment in minutes and alert the group it s complete and they re free to review and edit if they wanted only one member is required to submit so i do so and we get a fast forward assignment two is posted and group member b and c jump on it and complete it and relay the same message to the group blah blah blah they submit and we get a now for the drama assignment is posted during finals week and group member d says i haven t done any work yet i ll take this one and update you guys when i m finished great everything s going swimmingly he states he s finished and shares the file to which it looks good enough to pass a final brain skim through student d says i ll submit it however when he submits unbeknownst to the rest of the group he submits a file of the completed assignment from a previous graduate which he was using to complete assignment obviously this doesn t go over well and we re called in for ai we lay out the events and group member d takes full responsibility and makes it a point that none of us had any role in this i now have a meeting today with chair to review the fallout from this occurrence while i recognize the significance of this and i just cannot fathom how i could be held at fault for any of this issue as regardless of what steps i took besides personally submitting the assignment for the student it could not have been avoided let me know your take will update later tldr one group member completed a assignment for our group inadvertently submitted the wrong assignment and implicated him self for plagiarism however has taken full blame currently dealing with fallout while studying for final exams in next two days i hate group work especially for independent autonomous grad school professions', 'listen ye colleagues and hear my tale of woe i teach literature in a foreign language among other things last spring i was teaching remotely so i knew students might be tempted to cheat i told them as i always do they must never work from any prefab translation not even their own in class or on tests since that way they ll never learn the other language no surprises some students clung to reading off their prefab translations in class because it s scary to fly without a net and i reminded them encouragingly of why the policy is in place many left their security blanket behind and really improved in the class i discovered near the end of the term that one student had plagiarized his translation on a quiz so i checked out some others and lo and behold two students had plagiarized virtually everything the first student s was in very old fashioned english and the other two in modern english in case you wonder why i didn t catch them earlier quizzes exams you name it they even plagiarized the two exercises i gave them on creative translation totally free form no pressure grade for completion stuff yikes i failed them both naturally one of them went without a fight the other one is appealing and my god is he putting gusto into it he got the university counsel office to requisition my zoom class videos as evidence his family must have some kind of juice to make that happen evidence of what exactly the moment in class when i said feel free to plagiarize everything from the internet but i pointed out that these were class discussions featuring mostly other students who had agreed to be recorded for a completely different purpose i told counsel s office that i wished to know more of the precedent for using those videos as evidence for this guy to copy and paste for whatever purpose and that in light of this surprising requirement i d like to work with them and the dean of faculty s office on a senate announcement to make sure any other faculty teaching online know their class videos are subject to seizure at that point counsel s office conceded telling me of course i don t have any authority to require anything from you a small victory his appeal is in two weeks and it can t come soon enough my documentation is airtight screenshots of policies a document of his answers and the internet sources they resemble literally not an exaggeration and a helpful document with the of discrepancies laid out in a table so the hearing board can see they re all close synonyms furthermore we have a nice system wherein the board can almost always only issue a recommendation after an appeal that the faculty member change the penalty which i have no plans to do so i should be happy that nothing will likely come of the appeal but still i m aggravated this dude is occupying space in my head that should be thinking about my awesome new students how great it is to be back to in person testing and research projects so thanks for letting me vent here friends', 'sorry to hear how that turned out in years i ve sent a few cases through ai and there seems to be zero consistency in decisions at least when committee members change i ve never been on the committee but i imagine the definition of plagiarism is not absolute once two students turned in exactly the same solutions for a module that was maybe of the software they were supposed to write the rule about reusing code is you must cite the source and respect the software license ip the similarity detector i used flagged the code as very similar and since no sources were cited i sent the case to the committee the students showed they both copied the code from a book listed in the syllabus as a reference the committee gave them no sanctions in my head not citing the source was still plagiarism by definition in the university even if they didn t copy off of each other perhaps the committee felt the experience was enough of a lesson perhaps they had done the same thing when they were students etc either way i remember being disappointed especially after the zero tolerance propaganda towards plagiarism wasn t always backed up it s a human system']

Name: 39_visa_canada_usa_korea
Representative_Docs: ['i am working in the us on a visa with a foreign bachelor s degree but i have way fewer years of experience so take what i say with a grain of salt if you re applying without a referral i personally find you re much more likely to be turned down if you select yes to requiring us visa sponsorship and no to if you already have work authorization in the us however if you reach out to recruiters directly to explain your situation or get a referral you ll probably have better luck bypassing this restriction i m not sure what recruiters are exactly told with regards to visa sponsorships but you typically need a us bachelor s degree or equivalent to get a work visa online sources say an associates degree or in your case the dec is equivalent to years of work experience and years of work experience is about equivalent to a bachelor s degree however this is really up to the customs and border patrol officer to decide and companies may simply not take the risk to try to hire someone who needs visa sponsorship who doesn t have a degree the big companies meta amazon google and microsoft are typically more immigration agnostic they care less about where you come from as they re always willing to sponsor a visa you may find better luck working at a multinational company in canada and then transferring to the us personally i think your lack of us bachelor degree equivalent is the most significant barrier to you getting visa sponsorship and i can t comment if you ve met that bar with your years of experience otherwise it really just depends on how good you are if your experience expertise impresses recruiters hiring managers at the right companies they can pull strings to get you a visa gt i never felt like i couldn t do something in programming i m going off topic here but really i always feel like i can t do most things and the smartest developers i know say they have no idea what they re doing lol programming is so vast i would be pretty lost trying to implement a proof assistant implementing a jit compiler implementing a compiler in general particularly targeting an obscure architecture like arm big endian working on d graphics engines working on low level kernel code fpga programming quantum computing custom low level cryptography hyper optimizing code with x intrinsic instructions and obscure kernel features developing novel machine learning and computer vision algorithms porting said algorithms to run optimized on a gpu solving difficult data structures and algorithms problems implementing a self balancing binary tree off the top of my head implementing a virtual machine hypervisor with live migration etc the list just goes on and on of course noone should expect me to be able to do all of this but that s why specialization exists and why i can never say i never feel like i can t do something in programming', 'i have taken research jobs in the uk and sweden in addition to visiting positions in r us institutions in terms of the academic environment on the one hand there is plenty of freedom and stability if you are in almost any position other than a phd student they count as staff or postdoc for years then you become a permanent employee there is no need for tenure on the other hand it is harder to climb the ranks e g i am in a position that is loosely translated to research fellow it is a permanent teacher position what we call faculty positions with a split in research and teaching time assuming that i don t buy myself out of teaching duties but counts as roughly the same as an assist prof would this sounds great but if i ever want to become an assoc professor i need to apply for an opening and compete with external people if i was an assoc professor and wanted to become a full professor then i would need to wait for a chair to open or be able to bring sufficient external funding to justify said chair there is no publish or perish culture however having some high impact papers help with your yearly salary negotiation or when you apply for better positions you are expected to apply for funding as you are not guaranteed research time departments depending on their finances usually funds up to of your research time but the rest needs to come either from the faculty organisational equivalent to school in the us which gets its funding from the government or from a source external to the university there are plenty of opportunities to seek funding from both private and public research funding agencies in addition to access to horizoneurope cost chanse and other multinational grant opportunities we however do not have a culture of getting funding directly from companies and alumni in fact i haven t heard of any alumni funding ever here though recently there have been talks about starting an alumni database and newsletter so maybe funding will eventually teaching is funded by the state compare to the uk and even the us they are quite generous on how many hours they give you for tasks like marking if you are the unit coordinator then you can basically do whatever you want in terms of assignments exams scheduling etc on the other hand you can t just hire tas tas are hired through formal contracts after a lengthy recruitment process and are paid a normal salary this means that whoever is in charge of work allocation needs to budget a ta in your course at the same time there are times you will find teaching part of a course with other faculty recently in our ai course we were assoc profs me research fellow phd student and ta the conditions of phd students are both a blessing and a curse phd students here are funded and count as employees by law if you want to hire a phd student you need to find funding for them and they do not come cheap it costs almost k to fund a phd student the student however gets a good salary and pension it makes phd students be a more precious commodity compared to other countries and makes it harder to have those big labs you see in the us and uk on the other hand depending on your department you may belong to a research group led by a full professor even if you are an assoc prof departments and research groups tend to have a quite a flat hierarchy so it does not affect your day to day conditions living conditions it will be hard to beat sweden in terms of living conditions academics enjoy contractually flexible hours you pick your hours if you are in the office you are encouraged to minutes for fika at least twice a day plus an hour long lunch break you can also spend one hour at the gym or doing other sports and count it as a working hour every year we have lt years old lt years old and days lt years old days of paid vacation in addition to the normal bank holidays you are encouraged to take these holidays as you get a holiday allowance i e a small bonus for being on holiday in july universities are disserted as everyone is on holiday in addition we get almost two years of paid parental leave to be split between the two parents of course there is sick pay too however to enjoy all that you first need to deal with the swedish bureaucracy it takes months to get yourself sorted out the biggest shock is when you find out that people s addresses phone numbers and taxes are all considered public information in the interest of transparency anyone can request information about anyone at least with this layer of transparency we have a very simple credit score system more or less it says you are either in debt or not which makes getting a mortgage super easy most people own their house and most native swedes own a second summer house renting is anyway super hard as there is greater demand than supply in most cities finally there is the weather i found the cold not so much of an issue as houses are very well insulated and heating is almost free the problem is in the summer with climate change houses can feel like small ovens i also do not mind the very short days in the winter but the midnight sun in summertime always messes up with my schedule uk i will keep this simple unless you are given a position at a top university with a generous welcome package i would say avoid right now the uk government is destroying academia by pushing for narratives that academics live in ivory towers with fat salaries and pensions in reality academic salaries have remained almost static and pension schemes have taken a turn to the worst universities have also been pushing more and more admin teaching duties to already overworked staff', 'it doesn t matter if you re working for either a korean company or a foreign company in korea you re going to paid in krw to a korean bank account and pay taxes in both south korea and usa there might be exceptions but i think anything else would be rare the only situation i can imagine you being paid in usd is if you work as an independent contractor for an american company while living in south korea that would be a huge tax hassle that you d have to discuss with a bilingual korean accountant in any case if you re working in south korea you will still have to continue submitting american taxes every year exempting your korean income via the foreign earned income exclusion and also report your korean bank account to american authorities fbar usa and south korea have a tax treaty so you won t have to pay south korean income taxes during your first two years working in south korea but you will have to start paying south korean income taxes after that you have to file irs form in usa before you start working in south korea though in order to claim that benefit you will also have to pay into the south korean pension system although you will be able to withdraw the full amount in cash when you leave south korea you will not accumulate any years of social security in usa and keep in mind that generally you need at least years of work experience in order to get anything out of usa s social security usa taxes all expat income above swe salaries in south korea are about lower than usa though so you probably don t have to worry about going above yr as a mid level dev in south korea it s probably something you only have to worry about your final year in korea because when you cash out your pension it will spike up your yearly income if the company is big enough to sponsor your visa like samsung lg etc then they will handle everything for you visa bank account health insurance etc startups generally do not sponsor visas so if you want to go the startup route then your wife would need to do all the paperwork for you honestly the spousal visa is the best option if your wife is up for it because it gives you more freedom to change your job and it also makes you a more attractive job candidate to employers if you accept a work visa from an employer then it will be a huge hassle to change your job within south korea generally speaking bigger companies will have english speaking swe work environments and provide korean language classes for foreign employees without professional level korean fluency you would need korean language proficiency for management ai architect positions as well as startup jobs although it would not be necessary for entry level jobs at large companies if you re even thinking about the possibility of staying in south korea long term then you should start studying korean now because it s going to take more than a decade to reach professional fluency if you re working in an english speaking work environment']

Name: 44_papers_paper_literature_review
Representative_Docs: ['here are some toughts that helped me a paper reading is a skill that develops over time a significant amount of things in the paper are fluffy things that need not be read to understand the paper and only to clarify content or for low level details learning to spot what and what not to read will significantly reduce the amount of mental stress caused by reading b in the same vein read papers in three steps read the abstract if it sounds interesting make a rough read of the paper maybe like a minute read if it is really really interesting then you read everything at a latter date c i reach level in very fee papers in those i take notes then i usually try to discuss them with someone d seems you are in cs i have found than evaluation sections tend to add very little to the paper understanding so i usually skip them after a quick glance e just schedule some time to read every couple of days and take it as a light activity something to be listening music to only papers in the level are worthy of your full mental activity and of the slow grinding dull pain that comes from reading it at that depth f use a good paper manager medley or zotero once a paper is finished write some overall remarks about it these notes are indexable eg debates ai tunning of file system parameter uniqueness in their use of genetic algos really debatable the approach to fixing tunning the ai might be an interesting angle to cover evaluation is interesting could take a look at that for a paper background for auto tunning is very good get inspiration from it i write these notes at the end of a level read and serve me well to go back to the problem and remind me what the paper was and what was or not interested', 'hi everyone last year src i posted here about an app in beta that i was developing for academia that would make it easier to write and organize research notes called avidnote the first post i wrote on this subreddit regarding this was actually five years ago but at that time i didn t have the time to undertake it we have now finally launched the first complete version post beta with a lot of help from the feedback that you guys provided we ve had users from universities sign up and several universities recommending the app including princeton uconn etc the app is basically evernote but for academia it s meant to make it easier to write research notes and organize them the main features are ability to read and comment on a paper simultaneously by having the document that you re reading on one side and your notes on the other easily searchable and organized online this is one of the main drawbacks that i had with using physical paper to take notes it s easy to misplace and you can t really search your notes use tags to organize your papers for example by subject area author papers i need to read vs papers i have already read or any other tagging system find new papers through our search that is connected to an external database and import them directly to avidnote this feature is still being improved and is less reliable than uploading the papers directly one integrated platform for note taking reference management and finding new papers avidnote is not intended to replace reference management software but instead to compliment them you can import or export your references to zotero mendeley etc we have some interesting features that we are planning to release soon including collaboration and ai algorithm to summarize papers using gpt if you re interested you can sign up on the site for free and if you have any feedback requests or questions i d be glad to answer them here we do offer premium plans for more serious use since we do not have nor ever will have an advertising model all your data will always belong to you you can delete it any time or export it to your local computer if you prefer let me know if you guys have any thoughts or feedback d', 'as an academic i m amazed by the amount of time i spend on finding relevant scientific literature related to my work the process is extremely inefficient and although different search engines make it easier google scholar semantic scholar etc it is far from optimal not only that but many good papers are lost in a sea of thousands of bad ones it s hard to find them and it s easy to waste time on reinventing them instead of improving them our idea is to create a platform that automates this work at least partially in the beginning it would use the apis of semantic scholar and other search engines later we would implement an ai system ourselves we also plan to limit the platform to the field of computer science and later extend it to other fields in its simplest version the platform would take a paper as input and output a citation subgraph with useful information extracted from it a citation graph can easily explode to thousands of papers within two levels so it is necessary that the platform does some kind of selection and some filters are provided to the users for that purpose those filters would take into account parameters like for example the number of citations but without penalizing recent papers with high potential for example the papers could be classified into methods applications and reviews so that different classes of papers are shown from the selection of different filters for a given paper the platform would extract its hierarchy from its citation graph if only methods are selected the hierarchy would show the papers from which the method evolved from and the subsequent papers that evolved from that method here are some other things that the platform could do automatically find the standards used in a given field and compare methods results metrics benchmarks evaluate the innovation potential of a given field how much can it still be improved and how easily how large are the improvements in that field how many papers are published is the interest in that field dying or accelerating automatically create a summary of the implementation details for replicability purposes automatically find new areas of possible improvements allow users to open issues on papers as it s done on github and implement crowd review later i think that drastically reducing the time spending on reviewing scientific literature would increase productivity by a lot but i would love to hear your feedback on this thank you']

Name: 49_degree_college_years_education
Representative_Docs: ['i disagree with the notion that high school diplomas aren t valued because everyone has one that s not the case at all they re not valued nowadays because the requisite level of skills and knowledge needed to be valuable to a job has increased steadily and will continue to bachelor s degrees aren t worth much anymore they certainly don t provide a competitive advantage for most jobs on the open market in some fields masters degrees are starting to see the same trend our economy is more complex now than it was years ago and years from now will almost certainly be more complex than it is now technology has accelerated change that s gonna continue to happen without even taking into account the effects that artifical intelligence is going to have on the job market in years time side note i left the classroom years ago to become an ai engineer and hooboy people do not understand just how much ai is going to fundamentally change the meaning of employment although jobs that require interpersonal communication like teaching is generally considered to be safe nowadays not having a hs diploma sends a strong signal that someone is not a good candidate for most jobs but it s not a two way street creating an artificial scarcity for hs diplomas won t magically make them more valuable the main issue is that our education system is designed to create workers for a job market from the s if anything the most important thing going forward is going to be retraining i work in silicon valley and can think of at least friends or coworkers that are high school dropouts but make k salaries interestingly not all are in technical roles they command these wages because they re constantly learning and retraining on what their industries need they keep up on the skills they need and didn t bother putting time towards the ones they don t i m not advocating for this as general advice for the average student but it clearly works for some in the same way that the economy has rewarded these people constantly retraining and staying up to date with what employers are looking for it also clearly penalizes those that think they re entitled to stay in the same job for years until retirement such as factory workers or coal miners industries are disappearing and that s okay as long as there are programs to help those who are displaced to retrain and re enter the economy something the us doesn t do well at all currently historians and anthropologists like yuval noah harari have a lot to say on this topic tony wagner has also written at length on this topic the issue is that our current approach to education doesn t really do much at all to prepare students for the working world they re entering messing with the supply of workers with high school diplomas isn t going to increase demand for these sorts of workers', 'going to be honest with you i used to be on the path of getting certificates cfa cpa masters etc after getting a degree the reality is educational investment which may or may not have any return at all if you re here for the money education does the opposite and puts you first in debt if money isn t an issue for you then do whatever you want it s your life just don t regret taking or not taking the exam personally could care what people accomplish they re all the same some just live the same old average life like everyone else others question it and get out of it do some self reflection because you re putting so much worth on this exam no one cares really if anything it s a nice conversation starter i d be impressed but end of the day wouldn t care that s just my opinion do whatever you like though it s your one life just consider the time energy you re sacrificing vs real world experiences if you live in say third world countries please ignore what i said education is powerful in there and will help you in the long run if it s in the first world countries it s basically useless also do what your want to pursue don t live in your family s shadows just do some self reflection and really know what you want', 'i truly believe that the only serious life choices for a year bachelor s degree in usa and canada are computer science business related degrees business admin finance commerce included math statistics actuarial science data science included established mainstream engineering degrees mechanical civil electrical computer software chemical aerospace professional healthcare ex bachelor of science in nursing edit in addition to being in these subjects i also believe coop internship is an absolute requirement otherwise the degree isn t a serious life choice even if it s in one of these subjects this is in no particular order i think any other undergrad has significantly worse professional value and is not worth spending years of your life and money earning potential furthermore your interests can change during the years of your undergrad you may no longer want to be be a doctor or scientific researcher or lawyer for example in those cases perhaps if you want to be a doctor go for a nursing degree so you have the option of working as nurse if you no longer wanna do more years of school for those who want to get into scientific research maybe pursue an engineering undergrad so you have the option if in years life happens and you need to start making money or if you re no longer post only discusses bachelor s degrees completed as full time studies this post does not discuss college education or graduate studies what do you guys think is there a field of study i missed that you think has comparable professional value as the i listed']

Name: 50_masters_master_ms_cs
Representative_Docs: ['i wouldn t say master s degrees are scams they are designed for a different audience it is a good investment for some majors especially tech if you get a ms cs from a top university you have a decent in for a well paying job how else would you get an interview from google if your undergrad major is psychology yes it is still possible but infinitely harder than if you have a ms cs from a namesake university some jobs like machine learning engineers straight up requires you to have a ms yes they pay to do what you do but they aren t bound by obligations that binds phd students and they get their degree much sooner by the time someone finishes a phd in cs the ms student could be years into their career at google making k a year which is average salary for software engineers in nyc and about to get their third promotion that is a huge amount of opportunity cost that the phd student just paid the resource and network something like columbia can provide can also be invaluable if used correctly of course all of this hinges on the student being conscientious but that can be said about anything it would be a different story if we are talking about a ma in history but i wouldn t generalize all ms as scams ms degrees might not be for me but i am sure there is a lot of people that can benefit from it', 'one of my former managers actually my favorite former manager i ve ever worked for he gave me this advice when i told him that i may go for a masters he told me if i m doing my masters in computer science just to get a masters degree and just continue to do the work that i currently do and not pursuing a new focus then a master s really isn t worth it but if i m going to pursue something that i haven t learned that i want to learn and get more into then i should go get a masters in that you ve been working in this industry for years and right now based on what you said it looks like you re getting this degree just to get this degree to show that you have a degree to do work in this field i don t really see it as beneficial but if you intend on maybe going to do a master s in say artificial intelligence or machine learning or big data which probably requires a bachelor s then yeah sure go for a bachelor s degree in cs', 'this depends heavily on the quality of the specific bachelor s and master s degrees you re looking at on one hand there are some master s degrees which basically cover the same content as a bachelor s degree they re more targeted towards people who finished a non cs related bachelor s but want to pivot to tech on the other there are some master s degrees which have a high bar for rigor an emphasis on learning about and doing independent research etc you should research the specific master s degree you want to take and make sure it falls in the second group fwiw the advice i generally give is for people to not bother taking a master s unless they have a specific and concrete reason for doing so in most cases you re better off either a finding a job if you want your career to be in industry or b jumping straight into a phd program if you want your career to be in research gt learn new languages understand code bases algorithms i think the best way of improving your ability to understand code bases is to actually try working on some large codebases your best bet on how to do this would probably be to try looking for an internship or try contributing to some open source projects as opposed to taking a class a master s could certainly help you improve your skills in the other two more broadly a master s would give you more time to explore computer science more deeply this is mostly useful if you want to find a job where you actually do need to make heavy use of theoretical cs concepts things like robotics machine learning database implementation compilers distributed systems']

absolutely not what you want out of your ds amp a studies is to just be familiar with the mainstream algorithms and and data structures so that you know what you can refer to or even google when you re dealing with a problem that could be thought of as data structure x developing a basic familiarity and a strong intuition is what you re really trying to accomplish what s this i hear about object oriented programming oop oop is arguably the most popular programming paradigm what is a programming paradigm it s simply a way to think about and structure your code so that it grows in a way that is sensible organized and maintainable oop is one of many paradigms and it s usually the first programming paradigm most people learn first learn about oop its benefits and its detriments what is an object what is a class what is encapsulation what is a method what is inheritance what is polymorphism if you have a good understanding of the answers to the questions above i would say that you have a pretty good understanding of what oop is later down the line look into the functional programming fp paradigm as well as it is becoming an increasingly popular alternative to oop but learn about the problems that fp deals with i have a pretty good grasp of language x and i finished my ds amp a book course i want to work in web should i do back end front end or full stack i may get a lot of people disagreeing with me here but i think that it s too early for you to specialize in either front end or back end you definitely want to have a pretty good picture of the holistic anatomy of a web application in order to be a functional web developer but if you absolutely had to pick an area to be inclined towards as someone trying to break into the industry however i d recommend front end as i think front end junior roles are much more common than back end junior roles what should i learn next in that case to be employable well first and foremost you ll probably want to just get this out of the way now and learn html and css watch a youtube crash course they re easy to pick up and easy to google things for here are some popular web technology tool sets that you want to look into for a few popular languages depending on which one you picked in the beginning python django or flask ruby ruby on rails javascript mongodb express javascript front end library framework and node java spring php laravel go fiber now here s where my advice forks into different paths depending on what you language you decided to learn in the beginning for javascripters build a static web page no backend in just pure javascript html css perhaps make your portfolio website after that pick up a popular front end javascript library framework like react angular or vue and rebuild your static web page using said library framework my personal recommendation is react after that learn about the mern stack mongodb express react and node if you picked up angular or vue it would be called mean or mevn respectively build another app but this time with a backend and learn how the database backend and frontend all connect to make a fully functional web application note some people opt for using a sql database over mongodb but i m simply recommending mongodb because it s a pretty popular choice amongst full stack javascript developers pick whichever i d recommend mongodb for non javascripters build your first web application using the most popular web framework for that language and use little to no javascript learn about the mvc architecture or mvt in django land and what an object relational mapper orm is learn some basic sql and learn about the sql database you re using after your first app the next thing for you to do is to learn a modern javascript front end library framework i recommend react and build another application combining your web framework of choice with your javascript library framework you can find a lot of courses on udemy and youtube teaching you how to make applications that combine say django with react or ruby on rails with react all in all i think that if you follow the above steps and have a decent understanding of how the web works what is http what is the request response cycle what is json what is rest what s an api what s a restful api what is get post put patch delete what is idempotency in the context of say a post how does user authentication work how does data get stored how is data stored and transferred securely how do the client frontend server backend and database all connect if you have a pretty solid understanding of the above concepts i d dare say that you are probably job ready how do i know when i m ready to start applying if you follow the above steps and can answer the questions i listed i think that you should just start applying you ll never feel ready and you will always feel like there are a million things left to learn when people start talking about things like docker graphql aws or anything that you feel completely unfamiliar with don t let that deter you a lot of these tools and technologies can be learned on the job what s more important is that you ve demonstrated that you re smart hard working and have the know how to make a web application what should i do to prepare have a solid understanding of your own toolset as well as solid proficiency in one programming language with which to solve interview questions with do hackerrank and or leetcode to sharpen the problem solving muscle exercise to give the mind a break any other advice be hungry getting your foot in the door isn t going to be easy as it takes an insane amount of dedication and grit you ll likely find yourself getting discouraged throughout the process but if it takes you a year or two of hauling ass to eventually get your first offer the years later version of yourself will look back and thank your present day self for taking that plunge assuming you don t hate programming people are certainly going to point fingers at this and accuse me of survivorship bias or for being an american where it s easy to feel that your efforts are commensurate with your success but i m not about that defeatist attitude i ve been there i ve had my bouts with doubt i ve struggled but at the end of the day i wasn t going to let those become excuses some people naturally have tougher situations than others yes those people will have to work harder but it s up to you whether or not you want doubt to overpower impetus keep struggling friends and may you find your worth in the waking world the doll extra advice employed unconventionals early in their career if you re like me in that you started off with a very high level language like python and devoted most of your time to just using frameworks to build applications there are probably a lot of gaps in your knowledge about how computers and programs actually work if that s the case i would highly recommend learning the following learn a low level systems programming language that puts your more in touch with types and computer memory c c rust or zig just to name a few i personally recommend rust what are pointers what is stdin stdout stderr what is a file descriptor what s a kernel what s a system call what the heck is actually happening when i run my program from the terminal how does my program get memory to play with how does it know where to print stuff how does my rails django node whatever server actually work learn about sockets what is the osi and tcp ip model what are the responsibilities of the cpu what do cpu registers do what are kernel os threads what is parallelism what are green threads what is concurrency what is a data race what is a memory leak what the heck does unix like even mean what does the unix phrase everything is a file mean what is posix the above list is just a random dump of unorganized information that you can likely get away with not knowing for the entirety of your career but it is definitely super enriching to know and it serves to only make you a better programmer it is not comprehensive by any means and i am kind of just listing whatever is coming to mind good night everyone']

Name: 52_problem_code_learn_things
Representative_Docs: ['usually the two concepts beginning programmers struggle with are pointers at least in c and recursion once you get past the basics the hard part to me is managing the program itself it s like these event planners they have to be super organized and know what people need to be there what they need to do what stuff has to be there and so forth once your program reaches a certain size it can be hard to recall all the aspects of your program and most people maintain programs written by others so then you have to learn just enough of how the program works since some of these programs are enormous and it s not practical to learn everything as it s way too big to work with the program that s not a mental block like recursion is but more of a way to tackle code you didn t write once you do get recursion it isn t that bad it s one of the few things that generally gets to an a ha moment most programming isn t that way and just requires work it s like you work and work and work and you get it having said that you do need the right mindset to think about it so it s possible to bang your head for me it was useful to know how the code ran using a call stack but some learn it more intuitively i needed to understand the actual mechanics of how a recursive function worked which is still only part of the story there are things that can be difficult to learn but it s often not the language itself for example you might want to write a machine learning algorithm the ml algorithm is difficult but the language being used is something you know or a better example is to write a program that solves differential equations the hard part is understanding how to solve different equations the programming language is merely the tool to do it people fail to write such programs not because they can t program but because they don t understand differential equations the other problem is that newer stuff often builds on older stuff so let s say you learn java you learn it well then you learn spring which is a kind of library built on top of java now you are almost learning another language on top of java and you feel dumb that everything you learned in vanilla java doesn t seem to apply to writing a spring program this is even worse in javascript where there are libraries like jquery and frameworks libraries react angular vue etc that build on top of that and then you have to understand how web programming is not like vanilla java coding', 'the wall you re hitting sounds like the expectations versus the translation wall and giving up too quickly like for example you want to make x and some parts are pretty easy for you then when you have no clue what to do next looking up things feels like cheating feels like you re stupid and not cut out for programming the logistics aren t coming to you but researching feels like it s cheating honestly i feel the same way sometimes but i ve had to learn ways to overcome this the fact is you won t just know how to do something without a set of practices and seeing it done already despite how much you read and study for example if you d learn a real world language you d study words and grammar and some stock tourist phrases but when it comes to creating a new sentence fresh from your mind you stumble honestly that s perfectly normal the neural network in your mind hasn t been wired up to think like that all the connections so far have been made for stock standard phrases and words but you haven t created a flexible network that you can mesh together into a more creative and successful process i know people who have studied a foreign language for their entire life and can t string together a fresh sentence from their mind because they haven t created this creative mesh network here s some suggestions first i think it s fine to look up solutions you re learning you re not stupid you re ignorant you do not know and the way you learn is from seeing things done in real world languages it s common for folks to push immersion as in reading listening watching the native speakers use the language in programming it can be harder to immerse yourself you can t just read a long book with masses of code and learn from that programming isn t a purely creative process where you can throw shit out and see if it sticks there s set guidelines to follow so don t hit yourself for not coming up with a solution instinctively if you don t know the process if you do look up a solution imho try to skip over things to find the things you don t know for me i can t come up with the game mechanics for games when i did rock paper scissors in js i couldn t fathom the mechanics so i followed along with writing the code and after i took detailed notes and studied the parts i studied the program then i took a week off from that studied other stuff and returned to tackle the project again trying to use as little help as possible if i had to look back on the previous example i studied those parts again rinse and repeat and after a while you start to understand things second pseudocode often you might be given a task that s pretty complicated and it s daunting well break it up into parts and try to explain how those parts should work write out pseudocode for how it should work if there s something you don t understand do research it s true that programmers google a ton of shit there s so much stuff we need to wrap our heads around sometimes you even have to look up how to do simple shit the biggest part of learning is relearning pseudocode helps you sort out things you understand and things you don t know and then refine the things you don t know into parts you have no clue on third don t jump around just like a real world language there s that deep rooted network in your mind you need to develop once you develop that logistical sense other languages become easier to understand it s just the syntax that s different this is why your first language is hard to master your second is confusing but after that it s easy to fly through shit so i suggest pick a language you re most comfortable with and push through that until you feel you ve mastered not perfected it before jumping ship', 'quite frequently i see people saying all you need to do is break down the program into smaller parts and voila you can program i ve heard this back when i learned pascal and that was ages ago in general that is indeed how i approach solving a problem however this has to be taken with a little grain of salt in particular suppose i say i want to learn how to write a machine learning program that can beat the top players in go the asian game not the programming language first i don t know any machine learning second i wouldn t even know the strategy to apply a machine learning algorithm and third even if i knew those things it might not beat anyone it s no wonder that there aren t that many go playing programs breaking down a problem is nice when you know how to do most of the steps in the subproblems however this can lead to bad habits let s say there s a right way to code up a web site front end back end but you re stubborn and you want to reinvent the wheel and figure out things on your own if you choose to ignore what s been done then likely you ll never get anything done such as why is programming for web unlike standalone java programs why must we worry about http or javascript or json etc people talk about breaking a problem down but this also assumes you want to write your own code the problem could be a bug buried in lines of badly written code instead of breaking down a problem you are searching through some code trying to figure out what caused the bug it s not a problem of how do i find the best route from x to y but some problem someone got into by using a web application and maybe the web app is in react but you only know angular so now you have to spend time understanding react and the design and hope to find a bug not knowing what the application was supposed to do in particular you have to know your language pretty well suppose you learned italian cooking and maybe you used certain kitchen items to cook things but now you have to do indian cooking and the tools and skills and spices are different so the skills you might use to make an italian dish are no longer useful and you have to learn indian cooking techniques that is you now have to break down the problem in a different way a coding equivalent might be not being able to use a standard loop and having to use recursion to solve your problem often the challenge coming from a non functional programming language to a functional programming language some programmers will be looking for a loop and upset they can t find one they think in loops to solve problems and that tool has been taken away beyond working on people s code a lot of programming is more about talking to people most people do a bad job of describing the problem they encountered in our group we occasionally get these complaints as programmers we generally attempt to look at the code to see what went wrong but in reality it s usually the customer that had made a human error and we refer them to the people who use the application we maintain it but it is legacy and none of us built it and they invariably can fix the issue or explain the problem without us having to look at code some of us have to deal with such issues other programmers can be isolated from customers e g the team that wrote gmail don t have to deal directly with customers because that s too overwhelming to do so keep in mind that breaking down a problem usually implies that a person knows a language well and that they might break a problem down differently if their first language is java vs ocaml']

Name: 53_linear_algebra_linear algebra_matrix
Representative_Docs: ['linear algebra is used extensively in certain subdomains of programming but it s essentially useless to the rest of the field generally speaking if you re going into anything geometry related graphics modeling etc science related statistics related or ai machine learning related you ll probably need a reasonably deep understanding of linear algebra basically anything that seems pretty mathy to begin with will probably touch linear algebra to some extent amp x b on the other hand if you re going to be a web app firmware backend or most other software developers you ll probably never see a vector outside of naming convention in the c stl amp x b my recommendation for a student would be to not rule it out i realized in my senior year of college that i really liked machine learning courses and really wished i had spent more time focusing on linear algebra you never know what you might get into that said don t choose it over something that is more directly related to whatever areas of focus you re already prioritizing', 'let s take computer graphics as a great example suppose you ve got the x y coordinates of a bunch of points in an image and you want to rotate the image by degrees look up the formula for rotating it s basically gt newx x cos y sin gt gt newy x sin y cos now that s certainly not hard to implement in code but note that you could put the coefficients into a matrix like this gt cos sin gt gt sin cos now rotating x y by degrees is the same as multiplying the vector x y by that matrix above ok so what big deal now let s say you want to scale your image bigger too that s also easy to represent in a matrix gt gt gt if you want to both scale and rotate just multiply the matrices together now you have a single matrix that scales and rotates it turns out that if you add another dimension you can also translate a vector via matrix multiplication too so representing transformation as matrices is super powerful because d graphics are all about layers and layers of nested transformations you ve got your world then a particular character is scaled rotated and translated within that world then the arm on that object is scaled rotated and translated and the finger again and so on rather than doing all of that nested math as you go you just keep track of one matrix and apply that matrix to each polygon you draw want to undo a transformation that s the inverse of the matrix that s just one simple example of where linear algebra comes in the other common example is in optimization problems you ve got some problem you re trying to optimize in machine learning or statistics and you represent it as a lot of linear equations with a lot of unknowns which can be solved using matrix inversion or you re analyzing a lot of data that might be correlated you can use linear algebra to determine the principle components basically factoring out that correlation hope that helps', 'hi i am a canadian undergrad student in a cognitive neuroscience program and i would like to do research master and or phd in the domain more specifically my interest revolves around neurocognition of music psychoacoustic and anthropology and intrigued by ai shortly brain and sound before my neuroscience program i was studying music and social sciences therefore i didn t follow these mathematics classes physic linear algebra calculus i differential calculus ii integrals i would like to learn fondamental mathematic concept which will help me with eeg meg analysis and sound analysis i also want to get confortable in python and matlab it has been a while since i last did math other than stats i want to learn by myself linear algebra this summer as a beginner which concept chapter are the most relevant for me i m thinking of matrices and vectors as a first step but what else and in which order is there class up there that provide python linear algebra exercices apart of linear algebra what should i focus before grad school thank you for your help the wierdo']

developer if you aren t interested and self driven in doing all that stuff that s okay and perfectly normal i m not going to spend my evenings training ml to recognize a dog or a cat either and it s architectures for others it s the latest in large scale ai keeping track of people s money sounds so straightforward but making it still reliable that it s accurate and working of the time is Name: 55_ga_ec_machine learning_machine
Representative_Docs: ['must have been machine learning', 'with a sprinkling of machine learning', 'here ya go feel free to give it a spin yourself i d love to know what you think amp x b gt context free lindemann systems provide an important tool for modeling the dynamics of language evolution under selection pressure in this paper i present a novel technique for inferring them based on a combination approach of genetic algorithms ga and evolutionary computation ec the ga provides a fast and effective method for finding solutions which may not be found otherwise without any optimization techniques the ec allows to explore the space of possible solutions efficiently while avoiding the need for exhaustive search over all possible solutions this combined approach enables us to obtain very good solutions even when the number of parameters involved exceeds times larger than the number of parameters used during ga runtimes furthermore it does not require the use of a heuristic optimization procedure like gradient descent since the ga finds the optimal solution directly through the fitness function finally my work shows that the ga ec framework works well across a wide range of parameter values ranging from to generations where the ga runs about times faster than the ec counterpart']

Name: 57_ml_masters_experience_ai ml
Representative_Docs: ['what does proficient mean is the evidence of you being proficient you say so i don t know any certifications for ml that have weight the main things of value are internships touching ml or ml projects for projects the three main routes are contribute to open source many python major packages you can contribute research or just making a simple app built around some ml usage i m fond of pytorch numpy numba etc ecosystem and there s tons of issues for each of the repositories find some easier ones and diving in is one way one easier thing to start not to complete i always appreciate is improving type hint quality of the ecosystem as it makes ides much better at auto completion tensorflow type hints are very poor plus type hinting internals will probably give you a better understanding along the way and let you do other features if you want for research the easiest route is go talk to some grad students and collaborate with them my practical recommendation is to skip that and instead focus on smaller startups small meaning ish people contact them describe your ml background and ask for any roles hacker news has a monthly post on the st so yesterday where many companies including a lot of small ones have postings angel list linkedin search set to low company size also works they tend to be the most willing to accept low professional ml experience and then after a year or so hop to somewhere else that you want more i would weakly avoid going too small ish just for worry of instability even ish can be unstable but the earliest days of a startup are the most risky if you re fine with that risk it might be fun or might be too much work really depends on the team if after a while you can t find any place either consider going for a standard software engineer job and later shift to ml data science or you can explore the project ideas i mentioned', 'getting into ml is hard even for cs students it s the hottest topic right now and you will compete with some of the smartest phds msc from top schools over the limited positions not only from cs ml but also also closely related fields like maths stats and physics if you re up for the challenge though i d suggest getting a masters degree in ml cs if you can afford it otherwise you can try personal projects learn about sql databases and see if you can land a data science position it s often the easiest to get into i d say depending on what exactly you want to work on this might allow you to transition into a machine learning engineering role over time from there a transition into the machine learning scientist role is tough and will often require a phd although i ve seen very few make it without one in case you re confused about the roles i d suggest this talk by chip huyen there are also ai residency programs at faang that are specifically targeted towards unrelated fields these are on job training programs and the work you ll be doing is somewhere between the mle and ml scientist depending on your experience and the company obviously they are highly competitive since you re not only competing with the cs ml msc phds that want to break into these companies but also graduates of all other fields', 'you re not going to find an ai ml job not right now at least the industry got pretty creamed during the pandemic and the hiring frenzy is over so no one is going to take someone on that doesn t have project experience even if they have a masters you need to start working on projects and going after junior swe positions your problem imo is that you ve never had a job before worked on a team or shown up everyday to work on software projects aim start ups and mid to low tier companies learn a tech stack create a portfolio and apply to hundreds of jobs that s the recipe on a personal note i m just finishing up a masters in cs where my focus was on ml and some systems stuff i m working as a swe right now but the job market is a lot different than what it was years ago for the field the program i m doing omscs is probably diluting masters degrees in cs a little bit of all masters are part timers like me so gone are the days where your degree is a meal ticket to an easy corporate job i like ml ai and i ve used it before at jobs besides my current one but it s no longer the hot thing like it was when deep learning was still getting figured out granted ml ai skills can solve essential problems for businesses and there s always the potential to just start a company but the hiring isn t like it used to be the best you probably could do with just a masters and no other experience is a swe on a ml team or maybe a data scientist outside of large companies where you d get paid less but get the experience with ml at the end of the day you have to figure out is this career enough to motivate you to do the work']
