Name: 134_certs_certificates_certifications_certificate
Representative_Docs: ['none of the certifications themselves are worth anything at all the most important thing to understand is that what gets you a job in this field is demonstrating your ability to build things and solve problems every day this subreddit is full of posts from people who have been taking programming classes for months or even years but when faced with a new problem they don t even know where to start these courses will give you certain tools but the only way to actually learn to build is to practice a lot and that means a lot of frustration and a lot of failure along the way that said the general consensus on this sub is that front end development has the most entry level positions and the best chances of breaking into the field so these certifications are the most relevant responsive web design javascript algorithms and data structures front end development starting salaries won t be as high as machine learning or scientific computing but those fields have fewer openings and most of those openings are going to people with a b s or m s in computer science or a related field back end data analysis and info sec are somewhere in between quality assurance is generally considered a non programming career track with lower salaries and limited opportunity for advancement again what you should focus on more than anything is learning skills and applying them to actually build things i hope that helps', 'indeed it s hard to know what s legit and what s a scam and what people water down the value of by selling the exam answers cisco networking ones are famous and valuable not sure about the others i was looking at the admissions thread for georgia tech s online ms in cs aka the biggest cs graduate school on earth interesting to see the certs and training programs of admitted students who came from non cs non engineering background i ll list a few mooc certificates johns hopkins epfl deeplearning ai aws ibm georgia tech professional education certificate in data structures amp algorithms general assembly web development immersive aws associate developer and associate architect coursera and udacity certificates not specified harvard extension school graduate level certificate in programming', 'first thing first certs really don t get you jobs sure you can get a very basic job with ccna but that s entry level that is more a Name: 135_javascript_js_python_plotly

Name: 138_academia_industry_faculty_lecturers
Representative_Docs: ['currently finishing up a phd in theoretical physics in australia in photonics after covid happened hundreds upon hundreds of professors and lecturers in stem got fired from their jobs here the reduction in positions for research fellows or postdoctoral researchers in physics and math went down by over my supervisors won t be here after i am done they are in fact moving overseas this year the professors i had originally envisioned working with in areas such as electromagnetic engineering data science for astronomy or photonics got fired recently and are unemployed what this pandemic has shown me is the fragility of academia how people who have worked for decades in a field mastering everything about it publishing dozens of papers teaching and coordinating subjects and supervising student projects basically mean nothing to the academic institution unless they are bringing a lot of money it feels really disappointing to have gone down this career path physics has no future in australia and given my personal circumstances the only country i could move to is japan but finding a job there in academia is extremely hard especially when you don t speak the language after working hours a week in my phd for the last years including weekends and public holidays for literally no reward my degree is as useless i have come to the conclusion that i don t have a future in academia having said that i would like to know if anyone here switched to industry after their phd and how it went for you it s very hard to sell what i have done so i am thinking of applying for jobs in maybe finance quantitative or telecomm no idea what but i honestly don t know where to start do i need to go for entry level', 'looking for some unbiased input here i ve been working for about a decade and i have a faculty job i like my job but i didn t end up here because of a great blazing passion as i would advise students who want to enter academia i just did well in grad school a position opened up at the right time and one thing led to another things are working out ok but i m anticipating that i might need to move in the next two years for family reasons staying in academia is possible but it s hard because my niche is very small a few postings nationwide each year i could probably find a teaching focused professor job at a mid tier state school but compared to that the idea of transitioning to a more generic software job at a top company is starting to sound attractive to me all of this assumes that i m actually able to get a job and perform at said top software company and that s where i wanted to seek your input qualifications and relevant past experience phd in a top electrical engineering program faculty job somewhere between solo research scientist and tenure track faculty at a top medical school i work in biomedical research but there are definitely computational parts to it productive in the usual metrics grants publications that count in academia but don t count for much outside no open source hobby projects to speak of relevant cs projects wrote a paper on improving cache utilization for a computationally intensive algorithm that s used in my field i just did this because i saw an opportunity and it looked interesting it s not really my expertise other projects on using artificial intelligence for various medical tasks perhaps better described as applying keras tensorflow to medical applications rather than the deeper ai research e g at openai or fair i probably have the raw ability to do fundamental ai research but my intuition in that domain is still weak for lack of firsthand experience my specific questions how well does academic success correlate with industry success i d like to believe i have a lot of translatable skills but maybe i m deluding myself at the new grad level they say good grades correlate only weakly with good performance so is a good phd just the same story times ten i mentioned my situation to two married friends who work at bay area unicorns the wife mumbled what a waste in a teasing way with regards to my academic talent but the husband was very sympathetic in light of compensation and market differences where can i expect to end up i am guessing that i could probably with friends who could give referrals get interviews with several bigtech companies that i would enter one level above new grad tier e g l at google and that i would probably plateau at the normal senior engineer level that most do in years i think it is premature to assume that i would succeed beyond that unless i m in the top of that cohort does that sound right to you how should i prepare would companies still place me in the new grad category with my backstory in which case i should do leetcode and cracking the coding interview style books or would they treat me differently and expect to see different kinds of skills exhibited in my interviews', 'while i agree the job opportunities have slowed in post covid australia can you cite your source for hundreds and hundreds of lecturers and professors being fired i am a stem lecturer at one of the larger australian universities and we haven t fired a single lecturer let alone a professor to be a professor at an australian uni almost certainly means you have a permanent contract tenure i should have clarified when i mentioned hundreds of lecturers academics being fired i was talking nation wide and also at differing levels of experience including casual academics and research staff technicians this has been the case specially in universities across nsw but i won t mention names for the sake of anonymity but at least in my university i saw about associate processors in physics and math get fired despite having an impressive track record of teaching and research and having worked for several years there and i saw a few others from the engineering department meet a different fate a couple dozen lecturers and senior lecturers in stem were also laid off all within the span of a few months all of them worked in theoretical and computational fields e g computational optics electromagnetics nonlinear optics etc i also feel a bit of tough love is in order regarding your comment that your degree is useless you have a phd in one of the most prestigious and difficult fields in the world you clearly did not get to this point without being extremely bright and the ability to grind and work very hard use these strengths to your advantage the comments suggestions about non traditional jobs for phds in physics are excellent suggestions i m not familiar with your exact field so i m not sure why japan is your only option but surely your skills could be utilized in the us especially in the physics heavy california or new england although i agree theoretical physics is a very difficult field it is definitely not one of great importance in modern times especially when you compare it to things like ai machine learning data science health biotechnology computer vision and others this is self evident from the lack of positions globally that are specifically in the area of theoretical physics i m only in my second year as an independent academic so i m still very fresh to the way things are in academia my advice is that unless you re willing to continue the hour work weeks then academia isn t for you don t get me wrong i love my job but the perceived prestige of academia is wildly overblown you still have all the intraresearch group drama bullshit but now it s your responsibility pay is not as great as most industry jobs grant writing is a nightmare you still don t get a lot of your weekends just like graduate school i recommend to my students that you should pursue academia only if the most important thing to you is carrying out your own ideas and learning new things regardless of the pay and bullshit i agree with the advice i honestly don t feel any excitement from publishing research or conducting it so i feel my life will be miserable if i try to force myself into it forever']

Name: 146_calculus_understanding_prerequisite_physics calculus
Representative_Docs: ['i feel like calculus is on the borderline something which is certainly helpful to know in some situations and mandatory for some more specialised fields but you can generally scrape by with little or no real knowledge of it and even have an entire career in tech without really touching it directly i d recommend at least learning the basic principles understanding what differentiation and integration are even if you re not totally fluent at using them on complex equations in practice there have been a few times when i ve felt like i m probably missing a simpler more elegant or more mathematically correct solution to a practical problem which i might have arrived at if i was more comfortable with calculus not understanding calculus will lock you out of certain fields if you want to do fancy scientific simulation stuff or if you want to build and really understand libraries for things like physics graphics machine learning image processing etc and not just use plug in code somebody else has written then it s basically mandatory if you want to be involved in the academic side of computer science and not just the practical side of software development then you ll want to be comfortable with calculus', 'i understand that calculus and physics are used for game design and machine learning in between others however is it important to understand well calculus to continue a degree in computer science like for future classes i am passing calculus but not fully understanding also what aspects of calculus are actually used in software engineering i have passed calculus i currently finishing calculus ii', 'i have a bachelor s in mathematical sciences with a specialization in statistics and data analysis calculus is by no means a waste of time in data analysis if you were to take courses in probability and mathematical statistics you would quickly realize that all of those hours spent in differential and integral calculus were not trivial as for applied statistics perhaps the calculus shouldn t be a prerequisite that being said your understanding of stats would be limited due to a lack of understanding of probability theory which does involve calculus as for using calculus on a daily basis it would depend on the role and project if you were a data analyst working with phds who were developing advanced machine learning algorithms then you should have taken courses in advanced calculus differential equations on the other hand if you re working as a business analyst the advanced math may not be required since you would be using sql and excel to generate reports']

Name: 147_responses_grading_students_reading
Representative_Docs: ['something that streamlines grading and or assessment also by grading a process to simply enter grades into an existing system for example i currently pay year for a chrome extension that transfers grades from google classroom to our sis gradebook it s a little clunky but saves me a ton of time if someone could make a tool like speedgrader in canvas that works with google classroom i d pay for that for more basic tasks an ai that can scan simple student responses for a meets does not meet grade', 'i have a class thats using perusall and a good number of us students are having a hard time figuring out what the ai grading wants we need to read the online textbook chapters and make comments comment on others comments as we read the chapters and the grade is based on quality of responses and engagement which is all my professor will say does anybody know what kinds of things the perusall algorithm likes to hear', 'student one of my undergraduate history course uses perusall for reading i think the experience generally doesn t encourage intellectual responses to the reading or even finishing the reading friends in an anthropology class have similar experience and complaints about perusal as pointed out by others the mandatory comment requirement may sometimes tank students incentive to actually read or engage the text the comment implementation of perusall discourage long organized thoughts as it does not allow paragraph breaks this makes intellectual responses difficult to write and to read in shorter readings students tend to focus on rather trivial things to comment question on in order to hit the number of comment required occasionally these comments will appear so void of meaning that i wonder if they are generated by an ai sadly no someone actually wrote these poor comments may go along these lines asking the meaning of simple concepts or phrases mentioned but are irrelevant to the discussion of the reading asking factual questions that can sometimes be answer by a google search asking clarification of the text when he she clearly only skimmed the text being amazed that an idea introduced by the reading is novel to him her without further content or simply repeat what was said by the text e g wow i never knew that southern africa agriculture has been in contact with new world crops since s exclamations and or judgments from modern values without connecting it to the reading the discourse in anyway e g slavery in the th century is so horrible in xyz the combination of unwieldy functions and poor responses can be quite demoralizing for other students they may find expressing themselves unrewarding when overwhelmed by poorer responses and this may discourage reading others comments at all in longer readings you may see responses plummet towards the end or pages in as people appears to leave as soon as they finish the mandatory requirements admittedly my course is a level course with a professor who seems to attract a more chill students outside of the history department and the success with the platform will inevitably vary with the crowd of students you are teaching perusall works somewhat but there are probably better alternatives one alternative to give an example is from a course i had taught by two professors one from hist dept and the other spanish dept students are a mix from both departments the professors lends the course s reading from a multitude of sources academic and contemporary colonial literatures and arts and require students to post a word response on the discussion board they also assign a few students every week to read in advance and post their responses along with some questions this in my opinion works very well naturally the simple discussion board is technologically easy to use for both the students and the professors more importantly a longer more organized response necessarily require some engagement with the text i find that almost everyone provide some interesting insight to the sources the spanish and history students bridged quite well for the course and become complementary to each other admittedly this strategy will not work as well if the readings are more on the informative side less room for interpretation i hope this helps amp x b low ish level for the history department key difference being midterms and final test replace final research paper in lv courses along with other differences']

Name: 149_marking_assessment_exam_assessments

have also been taught that we only assess the skills knowledge set out in the learning outcomes your thoughts please and my thanks have super weekends', 'this is an automated service intended to preserve the original text of the post greetings professors i am interested in your opinions about whether or not every piece of assessment in the course of a degree should have a mark allocated for adherence to referencing citation conventions even if the unit s learning outcomes do not explicitly refer to academic integrity standards i am all for the clear teaching of good referencing and ai practices i have also been taught that we only assess the skills knowledge set out in the learning outcomes your thoughts please and my thanks have super weekends i am a bot and this action was performed automatically please contact the moderators of this subreddit message compose to r askprofessors if you have any questions or concerns', 'gt we usually have all multiple choice tests which would be significantly easier to cheat on they don t have to be a big issue with mcq are simply poorly designed mcqs and rarely something fundamentally bad about them potential solutions have a bigger pool of answers e g sample of them to present to the student shuffle answer order shuffle question order have all potential answers be close to the real one but make sure that you re not sampling a normal around the correct answer for numerical questions or else the real answer can be estimated by averaging gt i believe an ai grades them via latent semantic analysis i could be very off on this it s been a while since i ve looked at any of that do you think using lsa to grade free responses tests for s of students would be a good choice or decent alternative lsa can find latent concepts it won t give you a mark you could train some sort of answer matching algorithm but it d be ripe for mistakes as well unless you are just looking for specific keywords in the end you end up choosing between marking students or setting up automated marking for students running it and then checking and fixing automated marking mistakes been there done that my conclusion from having looked at the same problem and presenting it to my peers was the following marking is a pain but it s simply a symptom of over assessing you need to juggle the need for assessment students want to know how they re doing and the pain of marking one way to do that which i do in my modules is simply by removing almost all assessments and replacing them with automatically marked assessments which do not count for the final grade it makes my students happy because they can see how they are doing before the final assessment they get instant feedback and they feel prepared for the exam it makes me happy because there is only one or two real assessments to mark it makes my boss happy because my students are happy possibilities well designed mcqs assessments where you release the answer and ask for everybody to find a peer exchange assessment grade each other based on the answer this has the double advantage that you don t have to do marking and them doing marking based on your marking grid develops their assessment literacy which improves their grade for the final exam there is literature on this assessments where you read a random sample of submissions and then give them the answers with a general Name: 151_coding_teachers_teach_learn coding
Representative_Docs: ['yes typing is a prerequisite to learning to code but reread their statement theyre quite literally saying the reason that schools don t tech coding is because it s more important to teach the things that allow them to teach themselves my point is that argument applies to literally everything that has a prerequisite as someone who taught high school english before transitioning to the tech world and becoming an ai engineer i can assure you that s off by a mile and completely tone deaf to the reality of how important skills like basic scripting are for the current workforce are there jobs that won t require coding sure but the number of those jobs are shrinking while coding is in higher and higher demand each year we shouldn t teach it they can teach themselves is wildly off base i swear when it comes to new technology and shifting job markets a healthy amount of teachers sound a lot like monks whining about gutenberg s printing press', 'gt why does every want to get in programming programming is viewed as something that you can learn on your own no need to pay for school and has the potential to be very financially rewarding it s also a very interesting field that has plenty of exposure through popular media tech startups games ai etc gt if everybody gets into programming then what about engineers lots of people are interested in getting into programming but only a fraction of them will actually follow through all the way programming has an infamously steep learning curve and most people give up before seeing it through it s like being an astronaut lots of kids grow up wanting to become astronauts yet only a small fraction of us actually become astronauts the rest end up doing other things gt i have seen parents forcing their children to learn coding and there was a post in this sub presumably of a parent who wanted their year old to learn coding nothing wrong withe exposing your kids to coding i have done so however i agree that forcing kids to learn it is counter productive gt programming is really oversaturated by people nowadays i think that reality is way too far from that as i stated earlier wanting to learn coding isn t the same thing as actually learning it', 'i think there are a ton of smaller issues at play here and or big ones specifically a lack of teachers capable of teaching it and salary disparities that make it unlikely anyone that knows how to code will want to become a teacher we all know how criminally underpaid teachers are teacher pay is generally the same across all disciplines which makes it economically infeasible to be able to attract people that actually know how to code to become a teacher furthermore teachers that learn how to code often leave the profession burnout rates are super high for teachers overall and it s much harder to convince oneself to stay in the education system when they you have a skillset where programming interns make north of k a run of the mill software developer makes significantly more when i left my teaching job in fl to enter the tech world i literally quadrupled my salary overnight with an entry level position i can work from the beach and set my own hours i still volunteer at schools to get fix of educator impact but in terms of actually going back to teaching with all the low pay and high stress i can t ever see myself doing that i can afford to take care of my family in this job that was much harder on a teacher s salary until school systems make a concerted push to make teacher pay comparable to things like developer salaries we ll continue to have this issue there s also the issue of faculty buy in most administrators and teachers don t really have a clear idea of why bringing coding classes to a school would be beneficial there a few schools out there that understand what modern coding is and what a boon it can be classes like math but also english literature and art class it s a tool that allows students to create things that just aren t possible any other way i ll give an example here in i had some students th graders that were fed up with all ghr anti vaxxer propaganda they saw on facebook i encouraged them to think of how they could do something about it they got together did some planning and thought about how they could demonstrate herd immunity with a simulation they roped in the ap biology teacher to help them with their approach and point them towards information they could use in weeks they built a simulator that showed how a virus spreads through a population that is and isn t vaccinated they pulled r and mortality statistics straight from the who and cdc they even made it so that you could set the size of the population the of the population vaccinated etc it was absolutely glorious the biology dept actually used it to teach herd immunity the next year there seem to be a sizable amount of teachers on this thread that don t understand how modern coding can be used as a teaching tool i chalk it up to lack of exposure and dunning kruger effect personally i think it s one of the most effective multi purpose tools you can equip students with nowadays i use it to teach everything from linguistics to art don t even get me started on how different the economy will look in years thanks to the advent of ai the largest companies in the world are all ai companies right now tiktok is literally a product built by an ai lab to gather data it s not even their core product basic coding skills are going to be very important to the current generation of students once they enter the work force']

Name: 155_problem sets_music_apply_skillset
Representative_Docs: ['gt finish my degree in computer science since you re already on this track i think this is the best option gt just any general advice or resources that you guys can offer the main thing to bear in mind is that doing a degree doesn t necessarily give you the practical experience and familiarity you need to be a good programmer a degree gives you a bunch of really useful foundational and theoretical knowledge and at least some practical exposure to programming itself but the students who do well and are able to immediately transition into a successful career are those who spend some of their own time actually putting things into practice by working on their own projects it s like learning an instrument just going to music lessons is not enough and you need to put in that practice time to really become comfortable with your instrument take time to play and experiment to encounter and work through issues and to research problems yourself those are the real skills which you need to actually make software you can do whatever you want really trust that the degree will cover all of the necessary theory stuff and spend the rest of your time exploring things which you find interesting whether that s making little games mobile apps websites experimenting with machine learning in doing so you ll naturally build up that comfort and familiarity with using code as a tool to build stuff and solve problems and you ll then be able to adapt to pretty much any job in the field', 'there honestly is no road map to the field of computer science some developers learn on their own and get a job without a degree while other go to school or attend boot camps to gain experience on paper some go for doctorates and teach while others go into cyber security or game development it is really up to you how you want to go about this if you feel like you lack discipline then i suggest staying in school for a degree it s difficult to self learn if you don t have much discipline or an idea on where to start but if you feel college isn t for you then that s fine i m going to assume you want to stay in college for a cs degree find out what you enioy do you like the idea of ai and building neural networks to shape the future do you like building android applications or ios apps for phones do you like the idea of building web applications or going into cyber security how about mechanical engineering and writing programs for robots do you want to manage high quantities of data and do analytics on them it really is what you want to specialize into my suggestion is during your first two years of college while you are learning the fundamentals of programming languages explore these topics and more to find out what you are passionate about research the topics look at the job opportunities find what works for you and what you see yourself doing each thing has its own frameworks and languages that companies and universities use once you find your niche then dive in and see if you enjoy it as each year passes apply for internships really anywhere start with what you want the most then explore outwards it s much better to say you worked for an insurance firm dealing with crud applications than working at walmart during the summers this will give you an idea of what the real world is like coding it will test you do you like the idea of being stuck on problems for hours do you like sitting in an office all day do you have the discipline to work remotely etc by this time you should have a firm grasp on what you want to do in life so start out with this it might not be the go to java or python answer you wanted but nobody on here can really tell you what language you should do we all know your first two years you ll be introduced to a programming language or two and learn the basics so it s where you want to take that knowledge you learned and apply it else where if youre confused think of it this way if you learn control flow in a language like c like loops well guess what most other languages have loops its just a different coating over it on the base level so apply what you will learn to what you want to go into and you ll be fine edit i didn t realise you said high school at this point in your life just get good grades to help with scholarships because student debt sucks in the mean time explorer ideas and try creating basic programs for yourself in your free time a card game a java calculator anything really in hs i didn t even know how to really code the only thing i did was build websites with html and used visual basic for one app i did so there is no pressure just explore what you like on your own', 'to start off with just join the arts and sciences college of whatever university and take an intro class college is all about taking intro classes in a variety of things just to figure out what you want and broaden your scope even if you do pick cs take an intro sociology class just because actual questions do you want money and options do you prefer papers problem sets or nothing but tests what sorts of problems do you like to solve do you want to have the widest skillset or be a bigger fish in a smaller pond answers in order money at the end of the day this is a job i am much happier making figures than i would be doing a job i like slightly better and getting paid k for most of my life eventually you have a family and life goals that don t revolve around work so don t underestimate how nice a fat check is options cs is needed in absolutely every industry and realistically people tend to care just as much or more about their work environment than their actual work if you don t like your bro y tech company vibe go work in healthcare if you prefer the slow and steady proper white collar life go work for a bank they all need software engineers papers problem sets tests everyone does school differently a graduate level paper is just as hard if not harder than an os programming assignment depending on the student you ll find cs is firmly in the you ought to prefer problem sets camp since nearly all of your work will be programming assignments and problem sets your core courses will probably have exams but almost none of your electives will what problems do you like to solve i hate webdev so much most of the problems i was solving while doing work in a webdev focused class was trying to make two pieces of software play nice together when i finally moved text on a page a little to the left i did not get instant gratification i felt like holy crap why did i need to do all that for something so stupidly simple i preferred nlp where i most certainly don t get instant gratification in the form of webpage changes but once i do eventually get a piece working i felt like i d done something more substantial in nlp my issues are i didn t implement an algorithm right not my database not bloody connecting wide skillset bigger fish as i mentioned i do nlp i frankly don t have the skills for most standard swe jobs because i didn t take the classes for that that means i will never have to send out applications because there are nowhere near that many nlp focused jobs in one area the places i do apply to i m more likely to get an interview because i have a specialized skillset so the question is do you want to develop a standard skillset that can be applied anywhere but more people have or do you want to specialize in something and be a more limited commodity but have less options the concept of having that many places to apply to and just being a number terrified me so i didn t but that also locked me out of say working in healthcare or at a bank they might need general swes but they don t generally need machine learning engineers']

Name: 156_imposter_syndrome_claim_scenario
Representative_Docs: ['general background i am technically a software developer but i say technically because my background is unusual my undergraduate double major was in general mathematics and physics minor was in cs my master s degree is in applied mathematics my undergrad school was fine in each field a research institute probably ranked th or th in us my graduate school is pretty widely recognized and certainly top in my field i did undergrad research in computational physics not sure whether any of that matters but there you have it just in case more relevant background i have pretty extensive coding experience in research and academic contexts but i was a far cry from being qualified to develop commercial software when i entered the job market a little over a year ago that said i was hired on at a software company as a developer that specifically wanted someone who could do applied mathematics as writing mathy methods and documenting them is a big part of the job i feel very fortunate to have landed this position although feeling like an imposter and i think i genuinely am this isn t a cases of imposter syndrome has been a very real thing question my concern is two fold one some of the other developers seem occasionally irritated by developer knowledge that i haven t picked up yet but i don t know where how to pick it up except after that fact by googling what they talk about and even more importantly i m concerned that i couldn t get a job at almost any other software development company i think i m pretty good at coding but i have no idea whether i am any good as a software developer options i see work on my own full stack software project maybe build a website that does something interesting etcetera and hope that gives me more full fledged developer skills do competitions like those on kaggle since my background very closely aligns with machine learning and try to continue working in fields that leverage my background also it sounds fun grind leet code and other interview questions until i have a good chance of doing well on an interview that i do get i learned about leet code recently here but it seems to be a big deal something else that the wise and benevolent denizens of r cscareerquestions suggest thanks in advance to anyone who comments amp x b tl dr amp x b i try to include all relevant detail but this question could really be simplified down to the following how do software developers from a non cs background fill in gaps and make themselves more competitive', 'i m curious to find out since i tend to only hear about people overcoming imposter syndrome but never about those who were genuine imposters who left the field what do these people move on to edit to address some of the questions regarding what i meant by genuine imposter i meant it by someone who lacks talent in software coding and cannot perform at the same level as the average developer with similar amounts of time spent on training learning once in a while you come across something that might be considered as basic for professional engineers that you do not know which catches you off guard here are a few example scenarios to consider scenario you claim to know a particular language but google for syntax to use certain libraries scenario you claim to a software engineer and have worked on several small personal projects but fail on leetcode easy questions during an interview scenario you claim to have experience in python you have written scripts to scrape data from websites make api calls manipulate strings and store data in lists and dictionaries one day someone tells you to use a hashmap to store some data but you didn t know what a hashmap was or haven t realised that dictionaries are simply hashmaps you have always used dictionaries because it just works without knowing what goes on under the hood scenario you claim to be an ios mobile developer you have written elementary crud apps by following tutorials stackoverflow and published them on the app store but no one ever downloads them your apps crash randomly due to memory leaks but you do not know why when you show your code base to other experienced software engineers they discover you use an mvc architecture with a large controller your code is functional but does not follow any particular software design pattern and it has no unit testing set up scenario you claim to be a data scientist you have some experience with the commonly used python libraries scikit learn tensorflow pandas numpy seaborn etc with the help of google and stack overflow you can perform exploratory data analysis on the dataset you build your models by simply calling the standard algorithms from libraries with some understanding of when to use them you have gone through the ml courses on coursera and datacamp like everyone else you do not have a phd you have not won any silver gold medals in kaggle competitions you have not worked with big data tools like hadoop hive spark you have not written an etl pipeline some might argue that s not the job of a data scientist you rely on google stackoverflow for certain complicated sql queries scenario you claim to be a machine learning engineer you have used tensorflow pytorch and deployed models to the cloud with docker containers you have not coded backpropagation from scratch you have not published any groundbreaking paper in top ai conferences your work is derivative in nature by taking current open sourced state of the art models and with little modification train them on enterprise data scenario you claim to be a full stack engineer you have used html css javascript react to put together a basic crud website on the frontend however you have always relied heavily on frontend frameworks like bootstrap foundation material ui tailwind and made changes from there the attempted changes that you made are pretty much by trial and error based on targeting the class id of the element but sometimes it doesn t work and you are unsure why you rely on google stackoverflow on how to center a div if you were to write the html css javascript from scratch you would have trouble creating a decent responsive website some elements are out of position or look too big when viewed on a mobile device and you take a long time to resolve them you have not created a new reusable frontend component of your own eg a browser based code editor on the backend you have used node js flask django sql amp nosql databases s ec instances you have dockerized your web app or used serverless to deploy them on several cloud providers however the application has been written in a monolithic architecture you have trouble splitting it up into a microservices architecture while still maintaining security when someone asks you to estimate the server costs for a new project you have trouble answering them you are unaware of the potential drawbacks and scalability issues of the system architecture you have chosen you do not know if the rest api you have designed is any good but it works you do not know how to setup a ci cd pipeline with kubernetes and jenkins you only know the few basic git commands pull commit push branch and have never used rebase you do not know if the database design you have come up with is any good or if it is scalable amp x b i could go on with more examples but i think the post is long enough as it is i ll be more specific about the different roles in the future if need be', 'hello i recently wrote about my own success story i got offered my first role on wednesday this week and am still in disbelief prior to learning i never coded before although am definitely a computer person have a strong personal interest in technology i have a background as a traditional scientist non quantitative and have transitioned to being a data engineer in a non scientific sector in the space of months i learnt python html css and sql i mention these points for transparency i ll summarise what i did here months i was made unemployed by the pandemic i was told my contract wasn t getting extended in jan began coding in july on company time whilst wfh without them knowing and then became officially unemployed in august i did all my work solely through courses at this point some courses took me longer than others i didn t do any project work although definitely tried i d soldier through tutorials although wouldn t really understand what s going on and they would often run away from me my solution to this was i don t get this should i purchase another tutorial i was absolutely stuck in tutorial hell another problem i had was mindset because i didn t know what was appropriate for a programmer to take a break from follow along coding i would watch videos about programmers some of these were great explaining that buying more tutorials won t make you a better programmer the difference between active and passive learning completely demoralising how i made it into faang after months of programming for day dreaming how much do programmers make and for research what does x profession do day in the life of a x profession months i began building my own projects the first thing i ever built was a password generator as i was sick of coming up with my own passwords this then saved the passwords to a text file so i could copy and paste them the other projects were extremely unconventional i didn t want to build a tic tac toe game that a million other budding programmers built i would spend a lot of time figuring out how i could harass scammers who were emailing me a lot and also automate processes such as surveys as a form of making cash this then became automating a lot anything i was doing on a regular basis and then
